**⚠ The obvious filter is a trap, and the measurement is the only thing that shows it.** `fold_random` and `nested_train` are **independent schemes on one table**, so scheme A's random split cuts straight across the nested training fold: of the 2,354 `fold_random == "val"` records, **1,445 — 61.4% — are `nested_train`**, i.e. the model trained on them. Excluding those leaves 909, but **7 homology clusters still straddle the boundary** (some of their records `nested_train`, others not), contributing 12 more leaky records — scheme A assigns whole *clusters*, yet the nested scheme cuts *within* them. Requiring `len(context_seq) >= window` (so the full-context tiling never zero-flanks a boundary that is not a real contig end, PRD §6) drops a further 17. The honest fold is **880 records / 726 homology-cluster blocks / 37 orders / 35 class-II** — clearing `MIN_HELDOUT_POSITIVES = 20`, but only just, so class-II F1 **cannot** rank configs and the sweep ranks on the GATE-4 core elements (well-powered at 726 blocks). `disjointness` is **measured back off the emitted records**, never asserted from the filter that produced them.

**Evaluated under the operator that will actually be deployed.** Each record is tiled over its full context with `tile_windows` and reconciled by the ADR-0005 D3 + Amendment A3 operator (frozen in code, no config override) rather than scored through a single locus-centred window — so a config selected here is selected under the arithmetic P5 scans with. It also puts A3 on **real** logits for the first time: P2-03's golden could only pin synthetic ones, since no Stage-1 checkpoint existed yet. Full fold measured: **3,510 windows forwarded / 2,061,860 positions scored / 2 min 04 s wall / 2.99 GB RSS** on the 4060 — a per-sweep-point eval cost P2-06 must budget on top of P2-05's 1.613 GPU-h training figure.

**⚠ The reported `min_f1` is 0.0, and that is honest, not a result.** The smoke trains 4 steps on 64 records with a randomly-initialised head; it has learned nothing, and `is_science: false` / `gate4_graded: false` say so. **But a broken eval reports 0.0 too** — an off-by-one against `context_labels`, a wrong tiling origin, or a transposed window would all collapse the score, and an untrained model gives no way to tell those apart. That is the P2-03 lesson (an operator misaligning every left-overhanging window passed all 48 of its tests, because nothing numeric read the slice). So the alignment is proven with an **oracle**: a fake model emitting one-hot logits of the true labels for each window's own span must reconcile to `context_labels` **exactly** — measured **min_f1 = 1.0, micro_f1 = 1.0, every core element 1.0** — and a **one-nucleotide shift** of that oracle destroys it, which is what proves the test bites rather than passing by construction.

**Decisions.** No ADR (implements PRD §9.2/§12 + ADR-0004 D5/D6 as pinned). The ADR-0004 D7 training-fold guard was **not relaxed**: `class_counts_from_stream` still requires `training_fold_only is True`, because that clause is about the *training* stream — the eval is a **separate, additionally-evidenced scope**, which is strictly stronger than loosening the guard as first scoped. **Commit.** Authoring + result on `p2-06a-validation-ladder`; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.

**⚠ Three document conflicts found and flagged, not reconciled (CLAUDE.md §1).** (1) **imp.md P2-06 cites *"ADR-0005 (six-axis sweep pin)"* — ADR-0005 contains no such pin**; the string `six-axis` does not occur in it. The six axes are enumerated in **PRD §11:233** and include **LoRA rank** (moot: ADR-0002:129 makes full fine-tuning the Stage-1 default) and **Stage-2 aux-loss weight** (a P3 axis — no Stage-2 exists at P2). ADR-0005's real bindings on the sweep are **D15** and **D3+A3**. Precedence: PRD > ADR > imp.md. (2) **ADR-0003 D7 contradicts itself on its own freeze point** — lines 78/101 say **P1 first measurement**, line 119 says the **P5 sizing gate**; the Decision section is normative and line 119 reads stale. D7's number remains **unfrozen**, and it is a §7 stop before P2-06's submit. (3) **ADR-0005 has two sections numbered "Amendment A3"** (`:242` P0-28 magnitude rationales; `:273` P2-03 reconciliation) — and A3 is cited **by number** in shipped code (`reconcile.py` reports `pinned_by = "ADR-0005 D3 + A3"`).

## Step P2-06a (correction) — 2026-07-17: the selection fold was the headline holdout; re-carved from inside the training fold

**Inputs.** The same committed split/context/labels trio; the `is_designated_loo_holdout` column already present in `split_assignments.parquet` (splits.py:751, `(order ∈ heldout_set) and is_corpus`); the sound machinery from the superseded stanza (reconciled eval, block bootstrap, `select_best`). **Process.** Three independent measurements (one calling the real loader, one re-implementing its filters in pandas, one re-deriving `designate_heldout_orders()` from scratch) agreed exactly: the fold the first definition emitted was **778 / 880 = 88.41% designated leave-one-order-out holdout**, 687 / 726 blocks were LOO clusters, and its top order — Lactobacillales, 510 records, 58.0% of the fold on its own — is itself a designated holdout order. Root cause: `nested_train = is_corpus & has_order & ~linked` (splits.py:706), so filter 2's `not nested_train` means *"in the LOO holdout"*, not *"held out from training"* — and the fold's own `disjointness` block was true (0 shared with train) yet useless, because it measured disjointness *from train* while the fold sat *in the holdout*, and those are not complements. Re-carved `load_selection_val_records` as a **cluster-grouped seeded 10% draw from inside `nested_train`** (`selection_val_cluster_ids`, the identical rule the training loader now calls to *remove* the same clusters, so the two folds cannot disagree about the carve); replaced the `disjointness` block with a `leakage` block whose load-bearing clause `n_designated_loo_holdout` is **measured off the emitted records** via the dedicated indicator (never off a fold name), carried per-record on `CorpusRecord` (a required field — no default, since `False` is exactly what a fabricating default would supply); mirrored the clause into `_eval_val_ok` and `select_best.point_problems`. Also closed the three other confirmed review findings: `full_fold` was **written and read by nothing** → `select_best` now rejects a capped point (an 8-record slice's min-F1 is not comparable to a full fold's); `never_selected_on: ["test","loo_order_unit"]` was a hardcoded literal *demonstrably false* for the shipped fold → replaced by a `selection_hygiene` block re-derived from the points; added the `eval_scope.n_blocks == block_bootstrap_ci.n_blocks` cross-check the validator lacked. **Outputs.** Corrected `window_dataset.py` (`selection_val_cluster_ids`, `LOO_HOLDOUT_COL`, `FOLD_SCOPE_INNER_TRAIN`, `load_corpus_records(exclude_selection_val=…)`), `train_stage1.py`, `select_best.py`, `sizing.py` (pins the full 8,303-fold denominator, `exclude_selection_val=False`); rewritten `tests/unit/test_selection_val.py` + `test_eval_val_clause.py` + the golden fixture (real `is_designated_loo_holdout` column appended, 24/56 True; window digest unchanged, input byte-pin moved to `8989ddc5…`); a re-measured `reports/p2/train_stage1_smoke.json`. **Validation.** **Measured on the corrected fold:** 830 records / 469 blocks / 5 class-II carved from the 8,303-record / 4,775-cluster D5 fold (fraction 0.10, seed 20260717); **0 designated-LOO, 0 record and 0 cluster overlap with `inner_train` (7,472 records)**; training loses 10.01% (8,303 → 7,472). The smoke (seed 42, 3,334 windows / 1,962,499 positions / block bootstrap over 469 clusters, `git_dirty: false`) re-measures `min_f1 = 0.0` (honest — untrained), and the oracle-alignment pair still holds under real CUDA (perfect one-hot oracle → min_f1 = micro_f1 = 1.0; a 1-nt shift destroys it), so the eval is proven aligned on the new fold. Suite **1,574 passed / 145 skipped** (CI-exact, every anti-rot var armed) vs a **1,478 / 143** baseline re-measured on `main` `756a24d` = **+96 passing / +2 skipped, zero regression** (the +2 are the CUDA-gated oracle pair). `TBOX_REQUIRE_NO_LEAKAGE=1` → 25/25 (the committed split table is untouched — the carve is in-memory). ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 + snakemake lint/-n clean. **Compute LOCAL** (laptop RTX 4060; no GPU-hours). **Decisions.** The user's 2026-07-17 selection-rung decision was **re-taken** on the corrected measurement (the original rested on a false premise I supplied): promote on a cluster-grouped 10% carve from *inside* `nested_train`. Disclosed cost, accepted: the inner val holds only 5 class-II (the whole fold has 22), so class-II F1 cannot rank configs at any carve size — the sweep ranks on the GATE-4 core elements, and this is **reported, not gated** (gating a selection rung on a generalization min-N floor was a category error). No ADR (implements PRD §9.2/§12 + ADR-0004 D5/D6 as pinned). **Commit.** Authoring + result on `p2-06a-validation-ladder`; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate. Memory: [[nested-train-complement-is-the-loo-holdout]].

**⚠ DURABLE (the step's own defect, found by running the code rather than reading it).** The first draft copied the loader's **fold-level** `n_blocks` (726) into the scope of a run that scored **4** records — so the gate's ">= 2 blocks" block-resamplability clause passed on blocks the bootstrap never resampled. A field describing the *requested* population instead of the *measured* one: the same shape as P2-05's `basis_point` (a clause green on zero evidence) and P2-04's `/data` group (swept but never reaching the dataset). Fixed by deriving `n_blocks` as a set over the scored records, keeping the fold-level count under `n_blocks_fold`, and **cross-checking it against the number the bootstrap actually resampled** — recording a count twice without comparing the two is strictly worse than recording it once (the P1-15/P1-16 duplicated-`peak_vram_gib` lesson). A capped 1-block eval now fails. The probe that exposed it took six records and two minutes; no amount of re-reading had.

**⚠ DURABLE (a second one, and it is the §10.2 pattern again — mine).** The first draft of *this stanza* stated the suite as **"1,583 passed / 148 skipped vs a 1,528 / 148 baseline = +55 new"**. Every one of those numbers was **written before it was measured**, and every one was wrong; the authoring commit carries them. The measured truth is **1,539 / 145 vs 1,478 / 143 = +63**. Worse, the first honest measurement was itself **not apples-to-apples**: the branch run showed 5 extra skips that had nothing to do with the code — `test_coverage_sim.py` (×4) and `test_split_table_schema.py` skip in a **fresh worktree** because `data/` is gitignored and the DVC parquets live only in the main checkout, so a naive read would have reported a 5-test regression that did not exist (or, had it gone the other way, hidden one that did). Symlinking the missing artifacts made the two runs comparable, after which the delta reconciled to the collected-test count exactly. The rule the P1-exit gate already wrote down — *verify against the artifact, never copy from notes* — applies to **the numbers you are about to write**, not only to the SHAs; and a "baseline" measured under different data availability is not a baseline.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** No sweep (P2-06 owns the 36-point γ×lr×α grid and the ADR-0003 D7 sign-off). Nothing here is science (`is_science: false`) — GATE-4 is **P2-14**, on the real split. The two P2-04 handoffs P2-06 still owns are **untouched**: `report_path` is still unscoped per job (a `--multirun` would clobber it), and `class_counts` is still a per-record scan rather than a tally over the weighted draw stream (inert at the shipped `class_weight_alpha=0`, **live** at the α>0 the chosen grid sweeps). `eval_max_records` caps the fold for a smoke and records `full_fold: false`, so a capped run cannot pass itself off as the fold — but nothing yet **forces** P2-06's sweep points to run the full 880.

## Step P2-06 — 2026-07-17: the γ × lr × α sweep sbatch + the ADR-0003 D7 budget freeze (authoring half)

**Inputs.** `conf/train/stage1.yaml` + `train_stage1.py` (P2-04 entrypoint; the three P2-04/P2-05 handoffs P2-06 owns); `select_best.py` + `load_corpus_records(exclude_selection_val=…)` (P2-06a, on `main`); P2-05's measured per-run cost (`reports/p2/sizing_smoke.json`: 1.6129 GPU-h/run, ckpt-ON DDP×8, 10 epochs over the 8,303-window `nested_train` fold) and P2-06a's measured eval cost (3,334 windows / 1.96M positions per point on the 830-record selection-val rung); ADR-0003 D7 (the unfrozen sweep budget). **Process.** Authored `slurm/p2/sweep_stage1.sbatch` — a SLURM `--array 0-35` (one DDP×8 point per array task, deliberately **not** literal Hydra `--multirun`: one point = one node = one isolated process, so a CUDA OOM / NaN-grad cannot poison later points), decoding `tid → (γ, lr, α)` over γ∈{0.5,1,2,3} × `optim.lr`∈{3e-5,1e-4,3e-4} × `class_weight_alpha`∈{0,0.25,0.5}, each trained on `inner_train` and evaluated on the disjoint selection-val, writing one self-validated `reports/p2/sweep/<KEY>.json` + a `<KEY>.OK` success marker. Reconciled the stale `conf/train/stage1.yaml` "six-axis / window+RC" framing (window/stride held at ADR-0005 D3+A3 1024/512; RC-combination + CRF deferred to P2-12 per the code-emitted `swept_by`); recorded the two remaining handoff decisions in code — class-counts **record-scope RETAINED** (`weighted_draw_stream=false`; α is a fixed raw-fold inverse-freq weight on top of the P2-01 sampler, held constant across all 36 points, and `select_best` ranks on val `min_f1`, not `loss_mass_share`), and the per-step grad-finiteness scan **survives** to full-run scale (γ=0.5 is the fractional-γ regime P2-02 found a silent NaN gradient in, and its inclusion makes the budget a conservative upper bound). **Outputs.** `slurm/p2/sweep_stage1.sbatch`; the `conf/train/stage1.yaml` + `train_stage1.py` + `.gitignore` reconciliations; **ADR-0003 Amendment A2** (below). No sweep results yet — the best-config overlay + sweep-summary table are pending the SLURM run.

**Verified before staking a GPU budget on unreviewed authoring (the three load-bearing points).** (1) **No train-on-eval leakage:** `load_corpus_records(training_fold_only=True)` resolves `exclude_selection_val = bool(training_fold_only) = True` (window_dataset.py:813), so a sweep point trains on `inner_train` (7,472) and evaluates on the disjoint 830-record selection-val — the selection metric is not contaminated by the records it ranks on. (2) **The sweep is not a silent no-op:** `_cfg_from_mapping` runs the top-level dataclass-field loop **first** and the group loop (`optim.lr`, `tracking.mode`) **second**, so the sbatch's `optim.lr=` wins over the default `optim.lr` — a flat `lr=`/`++lr=` would have trained every point at 1e-4 while self-consistently reporting the swept value. (3) **`select_best` rejects, not down-ranks:** any point with `full_fold != True`, `n_designated_loo_holdout != 0`, or the wrong fold is dropped by name.

**Validation.** n/a on this authoring commit — the sweep gate (best config selected on val with block-resampled CIs; per-point `<KEY>.OK` + report validation, §9.3 artifact-based) fires on the real SLURM run next session; the authoring is verified against the three load-bearing points above. **Decisions.** **ADR-0003 Amendment A2 (user sign-off 2026-07-17, §7 item 2):** executes D7's determination rule now that both inputs exist — a measured per-run cost (P2-05) and a decided grid cardinality (36, user 2026-07-17). Per-run rescaled to the fold the sweep trains: 1.6129 × (7,472/8,303) = **1.451 GPU-h train + ≤0.065 GPU-h eval per point**; **frozen point estimate ≈ 58 GPU-h** (≈55 active / ≈68 node-reserved) under a **pre-registered aggregate bound ≤ 90 GPU-h** (headroom for a ≤2% D8 re-submit + reserved-node eval + the `epochs=10` *assumption*). The CPT pass (ADR-0002 D7) is **excluded** — probe-triggered P3+, unmeasurable now, re-freezes at its own first measurement. Fixed D7's internal contradiction (line 119's "sizing gate freezes D6/**D7**" vs D7/D10's freeze-at-first-measurement); reconciled the freeze point (P1's smoke never timed a full Stage-1 run → first real measurement is P2-05, enacted at P2-06); logged that the D5 prune lever is **not** triggered (55–68 GPU-h ≪ any real ceiling; ~8.5 wall-h serial on `two`). **Commit.** Authoring on `p2-06-sweep`; reconciled to the squash SHA at the P2 exit gate.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** The sweep has **not** run — the SLURM submit-ack (§9.3) is a separate §7 gate, not taken this session; no `min_f1`, no winner, no best-config overlay yet (all fabrication-prohibited until the real run). Nothing here is science (`is_science: false`) — GATE-4 is P2-14 on the leave-one-order-out holdout. The §5.1/CodeRabbit review gate runs at PR time (post-run, at step close), not on this interim authoring commit.

## Step P2-06 — 2026-07-18: the sweep ran (36/36) and promoted a config (result half)

**Inputs.** `slurm/p2/sweep_stage1.sbatch` at `4c723fd` (the authoring half above); the 36-point γ{0.5,1,2,3} × `optim.lr`{3e-5,1e-4,3e-4} × `class_weight_alpha`{0,0.25,0.5} grid; the P2-06a inner rung (830 records / 469 homology-cluster blocks carved from inside `nested_train`, fraction 0.10, seed 20260717); `select_best.py` (P2-06a). **Process.** SLURM array job **582** ran 35/36 points to completion; array task 8 (`g0.5_lr3e-4_a0.5`) died mid-training when rank 5 took SIGSEGV on node `one.amlab` — fail-close held, no `.OK` and no report, and the §9.3 verify surfaced it as a stop rather than an auto-resubmit. On the user's Option-A decision the single missing point was re-submitted under a separate submit-ack as job **635**, which completed clean (`.err` carries only wandb/torchrun chatter; the traceback scan over all 37 logs flags only the superseded `slurm_582_8.err`). All 36 reports were retrieved to the laptop, and the winner was produced by a new fail-closed CLI — `python -m tbox_finder.train.select_best --expect-points 36` — writing `reports/p2/sweep_selection.json` rather than by a human reading a number off a terminal. **Outputs.** the 36 committed per-point reports under `reports/p2/sweep/`; `reports/p2/sweep_selection.json` (full 36-point ranking, hygiene, cross-point consistency, provenance); the promoted overlay `conf/train/stage1_best.yaml` + `conf/optim/stage1_best.yaml`; `tests/ml/test_sweep_selection.py` (15 tests); the `.OK`/SLURM-log ignore rules and the `TBOX_REQUIRE_SWEEP_SELECTION` CI arming.

**The result.** **WINNER `g0.5_lr3e-4_a0.5`** — val `gate4_core_min_f1.min_f1` **0.9386** (95% block-bootstrap CI **0.9125–0.9550**, 469 clusters, 2000 replicates); per-element per-nt F1 **Stem I 0.9600 / Specifier 0.9386 / Antiterminator 0.9429**. 36/36 points promotable, **0 rejected**. Top five: **0.9386 (`g0.5_lr3e-4_a0.5`) → 0.9375 (`g0.5_lr3e-4_a0.25`) → 0.9352 (`g2.0_lr3e-4_a0.25`) → 0.9331 (`g0.5_lr3e-4_a0.0`) → 0.9311 (`g2.0_lr1e-4_a0.5`)**. Axis marginals (mean min-F1): **α 0.0 → 0.611, 0.25 → 0.805, 0.5 → 0.917** — monotone and by far the strongest signal; **lr 3e-5 → 0.533, 1e-4 → 0.910, 3e-4 → 0.890**; **γ 0.5 → 0.773, 1.0 → 0.774, 2.0 → 0.801, 3.0 → 0.763** — a 0.04 spread, i.e. γ is the axis this grid discriminates least. Four points scored a flat **0.0** (an entire core element never predicted) and they are exactly the four **α=0 at lr=3e-5** cells: the lowest learning rate with no class weighting does not learn the minority core classes at all. The one other collapse, `g3.0_lr3e-4_a0.25` at 0.5327, is the sole outlier that drags the lr=3e-4 marginal below lr=1e-4's despite lr=3e-4 holding all four top places. The full ranking is machine-readable in `reports/p2/sweep_selection.json`; no number in this paragraph was typed by hand — all were computed from the committed reports.

**Validation.** **PASS**, §9.3 artifact-based (`sacct` off): `squeue` empty for both jobs, **36/36 `<KEY>.OK` present with 36 matching `<KEY>.json` and zero orphans in either direction**, every report self-validating (`gate.overall_pass` true, finite `min_f1`), and the traceback scan clean apart from the superseded failure. The step gate ("best config selected on **val** with block-resampled CIs") is met: every point carries a 2000-replicate cluster-level CI, and selection ran wholly on the inner rung. **Leakage:** all 36 points measured `n_designated_loo_holdout == 0`, `n_not_nested_train == 0`, and zero shared record- or cluster-ids with `inner_train` — so no config was chosen on any part of the PRD §12:241 leave-one-order-out population. **Comparability** (a new check, `cross_point_consistency`): all 36 agree on `git_sha`, `env_lock`, `seed`, and the 830-record/469-block eval fold — a ranking across points built by different code would compare runs, not configs, and nothing previously checked that. **Budget:** measured training-loop cost **55.83 GPU-h** (sum of recorded per-step times × world_size 8; 1,160 steps/point, uniform) against ADR-0003 A2's frozen **≈58 GPU-h** point estimate and its **≤90 GPU-h** pre-registered aggregate bound — inside both, with the caveat that the figure excludes setup/model-load/eval and the partial GPU-time burned by the failed task-8 attempt, so it is a lower bound on charged hours. **Suite:** 1,567 passed / 167 skipped against a same-invocation baseline of 1,553 / 166 (`--ignore` the new file) = **+14 passed, +1 skipped**, reconciling exactly to the 15 tests collected from `tests/ml/test_sweep_selection.py` (the Hydra tier skips where hydra is absent).

**Decisions.** **The overlay inherits rather than forks.** `conf/train/stage1_best.yaml` pulls `train/stage1.yaml` in through its defaults list and restates only the swept axes; a forked full config would mean fixing every later Stage-1 change twice and shipping the bug in whichever copy was missed. Two mechanics are load-bearing and both are tested: `override /optim: stage1_best` (re-selecting a group an inherited config already chose requires the keyword — Hydra 1.3 defaults-list semantics, checked against context7), and **lr lives in the optim group and nowhere else** — `_cfg_from_mapping` fills top-level dataclass fields first and runs the group loop second, so a top-level `lr:` in the overlay would be silently overwritten by `cfg.optim.lr` and every downstream run would train at 1e-4 while every document said 3e-4. **`--expect-points` is required, not defaulted.** A glob matching 4 of 36 reports yields a summary that is internally flawless — clean hygiene, a real winner, zero rejections — naming a winner chosen from a ninth of the evidence; nothing *inside* a summary can detect that, so the expected cardinality is stated by the caller and the artifact refuses to be written without it. Each guard was verified by sabotage rather than by reading: mutating the overlay's γ, appending a top-level `lr:`, dropping `override`, hand-editing the committed winning score, feeding a 4-of-36 glob, mutating one point's `git_sha`, and reducing an empty sweep each fail the test that exists to catch them, and the tree restores byte-identical.

**Three caveats that travel with this result.** (1) **The win is not statistically decisive.** The winner's CI is ≈0.043 wide and contains all four runners-up; the margin over second place is **0.0011**, roughly a fortieth of that width. This is the top-ranked point under a pre-registered deterministic rule, *not* a config demonstrated to beat its neighbours — and the claim is pinned by a test that fails if a future sweep ever does separate them. (2) **The optimum sits at the edge of the grid on all three axes** — γ=0.5 is the swept minimum, lr=3e-4 the maximum, α=0.5 the maximum — so the sweep did not bracket it, and a better point may lie outside the box. Widening the grid is a new ADR-0003 budget determination and a §7 stop, recorded here so the next step inherits the question rather than the assumption. (3) **All 36 points record `git_dirty: true`.** Audited on the cluster before retrieval: the only **tracked** modification was `data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet`, whose working-tree sha256 `fb231fe4…` and size 4,922,605 B match the committed git-LFS pointer's own `oid` and `size` exactly — the cluster has no git-lfs, so `reset --hard` leaves a 132-byte pointer and the rsync restoring the real table registers as a modification. **Zero tracked `.py`/`.yaml`/`.sbatch` differed**, so `git_sha 4c723fd` does identify the code that ran; the remaining 41 dirty entries are untracked P1 run-signal cruft. The flag is pinned by a test so the caveat cannot rot into folklore. The CodeRabbit gate raised this and proposed re-running the sweep from a clean tree; that was **declined with reason**, because the remedy cannot achieve its own goal — the cluster has no git-lfs and no root to install it, so every `reset --hard` + rsync cycle reproduces the identical flag, and a ≈56 GPU-h re-run (plus a fresh ADR-0003 determination and submit-ack) would flip nothing. The concern behind the finding — that `git_sha` uniquely identify the producing code — is already satisfied by the zero-tracked-code-drift audit. **The durable fix belongs to P2-09:** have the sbatch record the *tracked-file dirt list* into the run, so a future report carries the decomposition as evidence instead of relying on a retrieval-time audit, and pair it with the input-artifact content hashes noted below. `git_dirty` alone is too blunt a flag to separate "code drift" from "git cannot smudge an LFS pointer".

**Disclosed non-delivery.** **These are selection-rung numbers, not GATE-4.** The 0.80 floor is not applied here and 0.9386 is **not** a GATE-4 pass — GATE-4 is graded at P2-14 on the real leave-one-order-out split; every report carries `is_science: false`. **The sweep reports record no input-artifact hashes** — provenance covers `git_sha`, `env_lock_sha256`, `seed` and the config, but not a content hash of the corpus/label/split tables the run consumed, so the tie between these results and the exact data version rests on the retrieval-time audit above rather than on a field in the report. Closing that is a natural P2-09 improvement, not something this step invented a field for. **Nothing was trained from the promoted overlay yet** — P2-09 owns the production run; the overlay is a selection, not a result. AUPRC is recorded per point in `eval_metrics.auprc_by_class` but was **not** the ranking statistic (ADR-0004 D6 ranks the core min-F1) and is not summarised as a headline here.

## Step P2-07 — 2026-07-19: hard-negative-mining infrastructure — the spare-rule gate, the Tier-2N probe set, and the synthetic non-canonical generator

**Inputs.** `src/tbox_finder/masking.py::spare_rule_excludes_from_mining` + `LocusIndex` (P0-30) · `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (element extents) · `data/external/refs/RF00230.cm` (`GA`=93.00, `CLEN`=224) · `src/tbox_finder/power.py::MIN_REAL_HOMOLOG_N` (ADR-0005 A1) · `labels.py::ELEMENT_COORDS`.

**Process.** Four modules landed — `mining/spare_rule.py`, `mining/hard_negative.py`, `synth/tier2n.py`, `eval/tier2n_probe.py` — plus `infernal.py`, the repo's single `cmsearch` caller (only `cmalign` had one before; `--tblout` fmt-1 fields were read off the pinned 1.1.5 binary because Context7 carries no Infernal corpus). `is_mining_excluded` **delegates** to the P0-30 predicate rather than forking it. Two §7 stops were taken and resolved by the user: the Tier-2N basis (ablate + verify, over label-by-construction) and the mining-gate backend policy (typed evidence + readiness gate).

**Outputs.** `reports/p2/tier2n_probe.json` (45 eligible / min-N 20 → PASS), produced by the committed CLI `python -m tbox_finder.synth.tier2n build`. Suite **1,652 passed / 167 skipped** (CI-exact) vs **1,567/167** on `main` = **+85, zero regressions**; ruff + black clean.

**Validation.** Tier-2N probe-set size **45 ≥ 20** — but only after the gate **failed on construct validity** and was rebuilt; see below. Nine guards verified **by sabotage**, not by reading; one (S8) **did not bite** and is recorded as such rather than claimed.

**Decisions.**

**The spare rule was vacuous, and its most plausible partial fix is the worst one.** ADR-0005 D14's pinned predicate takes three booleans defaulting to `False`, which conflates "the backend ran and this candidate failed" with "no backend exists". At P2-07 the second case is *every* case: there is no relaxed-architecture caller, R-scape is absent from every `envs/*.yml` (P6-01), and no neighbouring-CDS coordinates or strand exist for ADR-0006 D4's synteny test. Called with its defaults the rule excludes **nothing** — the guard protecting the flagship class reads green while protecting zero candidates. Fixed with three-valued evidence (`passed`/`failed`/`unavailable`) resolved as Kleene OR with unknown falling **toward sparing**, delegating the two-valued decision to the pinned predicate so there is one implementation of the OR. The sharper point is the **readiness gate**: availability is *not* fungible across the three disjuncts. ADR-0006 D9 row 5 defines Tier-2N as `(c)✓ ∧ (a)✓ ∧ (b)✗`, so relaxed-architecture detection is `False` on every Tier-2N locus **by definition** and can never spare one. A round whose only available backend is (b) is fully instrumented and offers **zero** Tier-2N protection — so the gate requires ≥1 of {covariation, synteny} and **refuses the round** otherwise, rather than degrading into a no-op that still reports success.

**The §10.1 evidence gate cut the generator's menu from sixteen variants to two.** An adversarial ≥2-independent-source review admitted only the class-II platform swap (`PMID:18359782`, `PMID:25583497`, `PMID:31740853`, `PMID:39097611`, `PMID:41290600`, `PMID:32882008` — five labs) and the glyQS joint Stem II + IIA/B deletion (`PMID:28621923`, `PMID:28531275`, `PMID:38981655` — three labs). Fourteen collapsed under **lab** independence once reviews stopped double-counting their own primary papers — three had two or three primary papers from a single group. Two are affirmatively contradicted ("no Stem III", "no Stem I") and have **no bin**, since an unsupported-but-plausible bin is a false-positive sink. Ablation is excision of *annotated coordinates* from real parents, so no sequence is fabricated. Consensus was quota-exhausted; routed down the §10.1 ladder to Scholar Gateway + PubMed.

**The validation gate failed on construct validity, and the failure was only visible from a control.** The first construction called a variant Tier-2N when its parent was CM-detected and it was not — a discordant pair, which controls the divergence confound (**27.6 %** of real unablated corpus records are already missed at `--cut_ga`, because `GA`=93.00 and TBDB was built with a different model). That yielded **238** and would have been reported as a pass. It is wrong. Every ablation *shortens* the leader, and a covariance model is an alignment: a **length-matched excision at a random non-element position breaks detection MORE often (78.8 %) than removing the actual Stem II or Terminator (66.7 %)**. Element excision is *less* destructive than the null it must beat — so the pair filter carried no architecture-specific signal whatsoever and was measuring excision. Eligibility is now a **triple** (parent detected ∧ variant missed ∧ length-matched control detected), each variant carrying its own control so one cannot be omitted, and an absent control arm leaving every variant ineligible rather than silently degrading to the two-leg filter. Yield **45** (21 class-II, 24 stem-II, both clearing min-N alone), partitioning the emission exactly: 161 parent-already-missed + 200 ablation-did-not-break + **193 length-confounded** + 45 + 0 unmeasured = 599. **193 of the pair filter's 238 — 81 % — were length artifacts.** Pinned as ADR-0005 **Amendment A4 (PROPOSED, awaiting sign-off)**.

**A candidate architecture backend was tested and rejected on evidence.** Before recommending the triple I checked whether the in-repo element-level `stem2_structure_only.cm` could serve as a real (b) detector: it hits **0/300 parents at E ≤ 1.0 and 1/300 even at E ≤ 1000**. Recording the negative result because the recommendation would otherwise have looked arbitrary.

**Sabotage log, including the one that did not bite.** Nine mutations: fail-open unavailable arm (3 fail), (b) counted as protective (2), unmeasured-pair inflation (1), min-N emptiness guard (1), previous-round halt baseline (2), unchanged-copy ablation (1), control leg dropped from eligibility (3), control window allowed to overlap the ablated element (1) — each bites, and the tree restores to 85 passing. **S8 — deleting the `cm_detected_control is None` guard — changed nothing**, because `None` already falls through `bool(None)` to `False` on that leg. Only the *variant* leg's `None` guard is load-bearing (there `not bool(None)` is `True`, so "never measured" would read as "the CM missed it"). The test docstring was corrected to state that asymmetry rather than claim all three legs are guarded — the P2-05 lesson about a false blanket claim in one's own test docstring, one level up from the code.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** The probe set is **provisional**: attribution is to the excised element via a **CM proxy**, not to ADR-0006 D9's relaxed-architecture detector (b), which has no backend until P6 — pre-registered for re-derivation there. The **natural** Tier-2N arm is **N = 0 by construction** (the corpus is 100 % CM-derived and cannot contain a CM-invisible locus) and is reported at zero, not dropped. The generator samples **two** literature-grounded departures; its diversity is a **lower bound**, not the natural non-canonical space. **No published RF00230 false-negative rate exists** — the §10.1 gate returned `NO DIRECT EVIDENCE FOUND`, with only the procedural fact that TBDB needed a second class-II CM (`PMID:32882008`) — so every Tier-2N recall figure here is first-party. No mining round has been run: `hard_negative.py` is infrastructure, and with zero backends available the readiness gate refuses every round by design until P6.

**Commit.** `<SHA — reconcile against `git log main` at the P2 exit gate>`

## Step P2-08 — 2026-07-19: the construction-powered synthetic-class-II recovery set

**Inputs.** `data/interim/splits/split_assignments.parquet` (DVC-interim partition, projected
through the pinned `splits.build_split_table`) + `data/interim/splits/aligned/class_{I,II}.sto`
for the D9 within-phylum-homology control. **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/synth/classII.py::build_recovery_set`
emits evaluation-only presentation variants (window-phase offset, reverse complement) of real
**held-out** class-II corpus parents; `splits.append_variant_rows` appends them to the committed
split table, each inheriting every fold, lineage, and cluster value **off its parent row** (the
caller supplies only `variant_id` + `parent_record_id`, so a caller cannot hand a variant a fold
at all). `tier2n`'s `_stable_key`/`_repo_relative` were **promoted** to `synth/_common.py` with
tier2n delegating, per the repo's promote-don't-duplicate correctness rule.
**Outputs.** `data/processed/synth/classII_recovery.parquet` (2,344 rows, sequence-free) ·
`reports/p2/classII_recovery.json` · committed split table 23,569 → **25,913** rows + regenerated
provenance (`n_synthetic_variants` split out from `n_external`) · `workflow/rules/data.smk::classII_recovery_set`.
**Validation.** PASS — block counts **103 clusters / 25 orders**, both ≥ `MIN_REAL_HOMOLOG_N` 20;
control strata `50-70`=1,019 / `<50`=61 / `no_neighbour`=92, the two low-homology strata each
independently powered. Full-corpus `tests/ml/test_no_leakage.py` **37/37** with
`TBOX_REQUIRE_NO_LEAKAGE=1`. Suite **1,756 passed / 162 skipped** vs a **1,680 / 162** baseline
re-measured on `main` in an identical artifact state = **+76 new, zero regressions**. ruff 0.15.15 +
black 25.11.0 + `snakemake --lint`/`-n` clean. Generator re-run byte-identical.
**Decisions.** Two amendments, both user-signed-off 2026-07-19 ("both approved"): **ADR-0004 A3**
(evaluation-only variants may be parented on held-out records; the `source` vocabulary is split into
`EXTERNAL_POSITIVE_SOURCES` / `DERIVED_SOURCES`) and **ADR-0005 A5** (the set supplies power, not
independence; the within-phylum control is promoted to load-bearing). **Commit.** *(squash SHA
recorded at merge — reconcile against `git log main`, never from notes.)*

**The step's real yield — the pre-registered construction was refused by the evidence gate.**
D9's "construction-powered synthetic class-II" most naturally means a class-I→class-II
**regulatory-platform swap**, which would have drawn on 8,281 class-I training parents across
16 phyla. The §10.1 gate refused it: platform modularity w.r.t. the sensing core is **contested**,
contradicted by **two independent labs** (Stem I/IDTM covariation — Suddala & Zhang 2020,
DOI:10.1002/wrna.1600; "class-II Stem I lacks several highly conserved elements essential for tRNA
interaction" — Sherwood 2015, PMID:25583497), and **no functional T-box platform-swap chimera could
be verified** (the one review-level assertion, PMID:31206978, cites a source that does not contain
the construct). Precedented instead is *specifier*-level re-engineering — a different claim.

**The fallback was a trap that a block-level measurement exposed.** Building from training-fold
parents satisfies "above min-N by construction" on the **record** count while leaving the graded
quantity untouched: only **22** class-II records are in `nested_train`, spanning **20 clusters /
4 orders**, and **zero** are Actinobacteria. D9 resamples at the block level, so 200 variants from
22 parents still span 20 clusters. The gate therefore keys on block counts, grades at the **weakest**
unit, and the report labels `n_variants` a presentation multiplier — the [[matched-control-before-certifying]]
shape, caught before it shipped rather than after.

**A direct conflict between two pinned contracts surfaced.** `external_positive_leaks` required every
non-`corpus` row to have `fold_random is None`; D7 requires a variant to inherit its parent's fold,
and eligible parents carry `fold_random` ∈ {train 1108, val 28, test 24}. **Every correct variant was
a violation by construction.** The predicate conflated "independent positive" with "derived row" and
had no clause for the latter. A3 re-scopes it and adds `synthetic_variant_leaks` (strictly stronger)
plus `unknown_sources`, which closes the vocabulary so re-scoping to allowlists cannot exempt an
unrecognised value from every check.

**Sabotage found three guards that could not fire, and one loose test of mine.** Against the real
(clean) committed table, disabling the parent-held-out, variant-out-of-training, or parent-not-corpus
clauses left the gate **green** — the predicates had no adversarial input, the
[[ci-leakage-gate-blind-to-runtime-augmentation]] shape one level down. Fixed with hand-built leaky
fixtures. Worse, `test_a_variant_parented_on_a_training_fold_row_is_caught` parented on a class-**I**
row, so the elif chain fired `parent_not_class_ii` first and the clause the test is *named for* never
executed — and my `assert viol[0][0] in (...)` disjunction accepted it. This is P2-07's finding
repeating: a test whose name claims a guard it never reaches. Now pinned to the exact reason, with
each disjunct separately sabotage-verified. Two further honest non-bites recorded rather than
papered over: the `bool(...)` emptiness guards in `build_report` are **redundant** (block counts of an
empty set are 0, which already fails `>= 20`) and the docstring claiming otherwise was corrected; and
a silent `astype(bool)` dtype coercion was untestable *and* would have hidden whatever widened the
column, so it is now a fail-loud tripwire with a test that fires it.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** No platform-swap chimera (refused, above). **Phylogenetic independence is
NOT supplied** — 1,160 of 1,172 parents are Actinobacteria, so ADR-0005 A1's premise that this set
closes the independence gap does not hold, and A5 records that. Nothing is graded here: the set is
scored Stage-1-only at **P4** on the P2-11 naive checkpoint. `nested_train` membership is unchanged,
so the 8,303/7,472 folds and the P2-06 selection-val carve are untouched. **Also recorded:** TBDB's
class-II model was only ever run inside Actinobacteria (TaxID:1760, DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaa1060), so the
observed class-II phylum restriction is an **ascertainment artifact** and can never be cited as a
distribution baseline — a live confound for the §1/§13 expansion claim.

**CodeRabbit round 1 — 7 findings, all legitimate, all applied.** Two were substantive.
(1) **`block_counts` counted a null `resolved_order` as a block**, inflating the graded quantity:
the reported 26 orders were 25 real ones plus the one eligible parent that lacks an order
assignment. Corrected to **25** here, in both ADRs, and in the report — a number that had already
been written into a commit message and a PR body. (2) **The test named for the block-vs-variant-count
distinction did not discriminate**: it emitted 9 variants, so a gate wrongly keyed on
`n_variants >= 20` would have failed it too, and the sabotage run I credited to it was actually
being caught by `test_the_gate_grades_at_the_weakest_block_unit`. Now 45 variants inside one block,
and re-sabotage confirms it fails **in its own name**. That is the second time this step that a guard
passed while a *different* guard did the work — my sabotage attribution, not just my tests, needs to
name which assertion fired. Also applied: `stable_key` now rejects the `|` delimiter rather than
letting `("a|b",)` and `("a","b")` key identically (no committed artifact changes — no record id
contains `|`); `repo_relative` searches the path's own ancestry before cwd's, so an artifact outside
the working directory keeps its directory component instead of degrading to a basename;
`validate_report` is total against a non-mapping root and against non-int block counts / `n_parents`;
and the Snakemake rule now **declares** the two `.sto` alignment inputs, so the D9 control cannot
silently report "unmeasured" because an undeclared input was never built. Suite **1,764 / 162**.


## Step P2-09 — 2026-07-19: the round-0 production Stage-1 scanner (authoring — SLURM submit-ack pending)

**Inputs.** The three corpus parquets (`context_v0`, `labels_v0`, `split_assignments`), the pinned
Caduceus-PS backbone (`kuleshov-group/caduceus-ps_seqlen-131k_d_model-256_n_layer-16` @ `d89eeb85`),
and the P2-06-promoted winner config `g0.5_lr3e-4_a0.5` (γ=0.5, `optim.lr`=3e-4, `class_weight_alpha`=0.5).
**Process.** `slurm/p2/train_production.sbatch` drives the real P2-04 entrypoint
(`src/tbox_finder/train/train_stage1.py`) once, DDP×8, over the **full ADR-0004 D5 fold** (8,303
records) with `save_checkpoint=true`. The step lands the sbatch + the code the full-fold retrain
needs: a new `exclude_selection_val` config field threaded to `load_corpus_records`, its
`__post_init__` guard, a hardened eval clause, and the tests — the checkpoint itself is produced only
after the submit-ack. **Outputs (this commit).** `slurm/p2/train_production.sbatch` ·
`train_stage1.py` (`exclude_selection_val`, the config guard, the `class_counts_scope.selection_val_excluded`
cross-check in `_eval_val_ok`) · `tests/unit/test_production_fold.py` (9) + extended
`tests/unit/test_eval_val_clause.py` + the `tests/ml/test_train_smoke.py` fixture. **Outputs (post-run,
follow-up commit).** `data/processed/checkpoints/stage1_production/stage1.pt` (DVC-added) +
`provenance.json`; `reports/p2/train_stage1_production.json`; W&B (offline) run id.

**Two decisions (user sign-off 2026-07-19).** (1) **Full 8,303 D5 fold, no val metric reported.** A
sweep must hold `selection_val` out of its points' gradients; once the config is fixed that reason is
spent, so the shipped checkpoint trains the whole fold (`exclude_selection_val=false`). There is then
no free held-out rung — the LOO holdout is P2-14's GATE-4 grading set (using it here would contaminate
the §12 headline), and `selection_val` is now inside training — so the run reports **no** val number
(`eval_val=false`) rather than an in-sample one. imp.md's "held-out val F1 reported" gate is therefore
**met by refusal**, with the reason recorded. (2) **fp32, TF32 off — as ADR-0002 A7 pins, not bf16.**
imp.md's Outputs line says "bf16/TF32", but no PRD line or ADR pins bf16 for Stage-1: PRD §10.3's
"bf16/TF32 native" describes the A4000 *card*, and A7 disables TF32 for determinism (a re-enable moves
metrics ≥1e-2). `train_stage1` exposes no precision knob; adding bf16 would need an ADR-0002 amendment.
imp.md's wording is descriptive drift, flagged here for correction, not a pin.

**The trap the code closes (the step's real yield).** `exclude_selection_val=false` + `eval_val=true`
trains on the val fold and then scores it — and the disjointness evidence would **still read zero**,
because `load_selection_val_records` measures overlap against the `inner_train` fold *definition*
(window_dataset.py:948-957), which is disjoint from `selection_val` by construction however the
optimiser was fed. The gate would go green on an in-sample number labelled `eval_split: selection_val`
under a leakage block asserting disjointness — the P2-06a defect in a new costume: a total,
evidence-guarded clause measuring the wrong population ([[gate-clauses-need-re-derivation]],
[[nested-train-complement-is-the-loo-holdout]]). Closed at two levels: `Stage1TrainConfig.__post_init__`
refuses the combination at construction (unreachable from Hydra), and `_eval_val_ok` now cross-checks
the training scope's own `selection_val_excluded` flag so a hand-assembled or regenerated report that
bypasses the config still fails. Both guards **sabotage-verified to bite in their own name**.

**Validation.** Authoring gate: unit suite **1,426 passed / 156 skipped** (bare CI-exact) with **zero
regressions** (the 2 `test_split_table_schema` failures reproduce identically on clean `main` — a
pre-existing local NaN-comparison in the data state, not P2-09); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean;
the new clause + config guard each proven by sabotage. The **run** gate (DONE marker + self-validated
report + checkpoint DVC-added) fires only after the SLURM submit-ack. **Compute** SLURM
(`--gres=gpu:a4000:8`); measured est ≈ **1.8 GPU-h / ~0.23 h wall** (the P2-06 winner ran 1,160 steps
@ 0.605 s median at this exact shape; scaled by 8303/7472). **Commit.** authoring SHA pending.

## Step P2-09a — 2026-07-20: job 669 died on an un-composable override; the sbatch-override gate

**Inputs.** SLURM job 669 (submitted 2026-07-19T15:04:49), `slurm/p2/train_production.sbatch`,
`conf/train/stage1.yaml`, `reports/p2/train_production_669.{out,err}`.

**What happened.** 669 sat `PENDING (Resources)` for **14 h 36 m** on the cryosparc-fragmented `gpu`
partition, started at 05:41, and was dead by 05:43 — every rank on::

    Could not override 'exclude_selection_val'.
    Key 'exclude_selection_val' is not in struct

P2-09 added `exclude_selection_val` as a `Stage1TrainConfig` field (train_stage1.py:181) and passed it
on the `torchrun` line, but never added the key to `conf/train/stage1.yaml`. Hydra composes in **struct
mode**, so an override naming an absent key is a hard `ConfigCompositionException` raised before any
model is built. No GPU-hours were consumed; the cost was the queue wait. Everything upstream worked
exactly as designed — all three parquets staged and hash-verified (`split_assignments` sha256
`6388ba19…` = the committed LFS oid), 8×A4000 visible, `selective_scan_cuda` OK, HF cache warm — and
the CodeRabbit-r1 reorder (stale-output `rm -f` moved *after* the staged-input asserts) meant the
failure destroyed nothing.

**Why the suite did not catch it.** `tests/unit/test_production_fold.py` exercises
`_cfg_from_mapping({"exclude_selection_val": False, …})` — a **plain dict, one layer below Hydra** — and
`sbatch --test-only` validates SLURM resources, never the command. The previous stanza records both
P2-09 guards as "sabotage-verified to bite in their own name", and they were: at the *dataclass* and
*report* layers. The composition layer between the shipped file and those guards was untested by
anything. Quantified: with the config key removed — the exact defect — the pre-existing suite still
reports **1,774 passed, 0 failed**. All 1,774 tests were blind to it. This is
[[verify-the-line-you-ship]] again, one layer up: the pre-ack probe checked imports and staged inputs,
never the override tokens the artifact actually ships.

**Process.** Reproduced locally in the `tbox-finder-data` env (hydra 1.3.2) by composing
`train/stage1` with the override parsed out of the sbatch's own bytes. Fixed by adding
`exclude_selection_val: true` to `conf/train/stage1.yaml` — default `true` = the `inner_train` fold,
matching the dataclass default, so only P2-09's explicit `false` changes behaviour.

**Outputs.** `conf/train/stage1.yaml` (+ the key and its trap note) ·
`tests/unit/test_sbatch_overrides.py` (**10 tests**) — reads every `slurm/**/*.sbatch`, extracts the
override tokens after `-m <module>`, resolves each entrypoint's `config_name` by statically parsing its
`@hydra.main` decorator (no import, no torch), and runs **real struct-mode composition**; 6 launch
blocks across 5 files are gated · `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (+`hydra-core==1.3.2`,
`omegaconf==2.3.0`, both == `envs/data.yml`) so the gate cannot `importorskip` green in CI.

**A second hole, found while authoring.** The first cut keyed discovery off launcher names
(`torchrun`/`python -m`) and **silently dropped** `slurm/p1/gtdb_continued_pretrain.sbatch`, which uses
`accelerate launch` — a Hydra entrypoint with three ungated overrides. Discovery is now
launcher-agnostic (`-m tbox_finder.…`), and `test_no_sbatch_launch_is_silently_dropped` asserts
file-level coverage directly, so a fourth launcher cannot quietly shrink the gate.

**Validation.** CI-exact **1,784 passed / 162 skipped**, against a **1,774**-passed baseline measured
by the same command ignoring only the new file = **+10, zero regressions**; ruff 0.15.15 + black
25.11.0 clean. Two sabotages, each naming its test ([[sabotage-attribution-names-the-test]]):
removing the config key failed exactly `test_every_sbatch_override_composes[…train_production…]` +
`test_exclude_selection_val_is_a_config_key_and_a_dataclass_field` (8 others still passing — a specific
bite, not a blanket one); reverting discovery to launcher-names failed exactly
`test_no_sbatch_launch_is_silently_dropped`, and the collected count fell 10 → 9 as the GTDB case
vanished — the silent-drop signature made visible.

**Decisions.** No sbatch change: the launch line was correct and the config was wrong, so fixing the
config keeps the run shape (DDP×8, PRD §10.3) and the P2-06-selected config untouched. Re-submitting
needs a fresh §9.3 ack — the changed tree voids the job-test 667 preflight. **Commit.** SHA pending.

**Review (CodeRabbit r3, 2 minor — both applied).** (1) The dataclass-field assertion regexed the
module text, so an indented `exclude_selection_val: bool` *anywhere* — another dataclass, a local
annotation — would satisfy it while `Stage1TrainConfig` itself had lost the field; now parsed via
`ast` and scoped to that class's own annotations (AST, not import: the module pulls in torch).
(2) The `TBOX_REQUIRE_TRAIN_HYDRA` / `TBOX_REQUIRE_SWEEP_HYDRA` comments still read "CI installs no
hydra" — stale the moment hydra was added. Chasing that flag found a **second, larger hole**: those two
tiers had been **skipping silently in every CI run since P2-04** — `tests/ml/test_train_smoke.py`'s
four composition tests plus `tests/ml/test_sweep_selection.py`'s overlay test, five tests, green by
omission. Both vars are now armed. Verified the contract bites rather than decorating: with hydra
absent and the vars armed the five FAIL loudly (`5 failed, 68 passed`); absent and unarmed they go
`68 passed, 12 skipped` — the exact silent green CI had been reporting.

## Step P2-09 (run) — 2026-07-20: job 671 — the round-0 production Stage-1 checkpoint

**Inputs.** The three staged corpus parquets (re-staged after the cluster re-sync reverted
`split_assignments.parquet` to a 132-byte LFS pointer; sha256 `6388ba19…` = committed oid, size
5073699) · `slurm/p2/train_production.sbatch` @ `0f8d527`.

**Process.** Submitted under the §9.3 ack after a pre-ack probe that **composed the sbatch's own
override tokens in `tbox-ml-dna` on the cluster checkout** — the check whose absence cost job 669.
Node `two` had gone fully idle (the cryosparc pair holding 8/8 ended after ~14 h), so 671 started
immediately and ran **14 m 12 s** wall, DDP×8.

**Outputs.** `data/processed/checkpoints/stage1_production/{stage1.pt,provenance.json}` (DVC-tracked,
`stage1_production.dvc`, md5 `c1b5d244…dir`, 2 files / 31,038,761 B) ·
`reports/p2/train_stage1_production.json` (git-tracked) · `reports/p2/train_stage1_production.DONE`.

**Validation — artifact-based (`sacct` off, §9.3 step 8).** (a) `squeue -j 671` empty; (b) DONE
present; (c) checkpoint + sidecar present; (d) report `gate.overall_pass = true` with all seven
clauses true (`train_step_ran`, `loss_finite`, `grads_finite`, `class_counts_from_stream`,
`gradient_checkpointing_applied`, `provenance_complete`, `eval_val_scored_on_disjoint_fold`);
(e) `.err` **897 B — expected non-empty** (W&B-offline chatter), scanned for
`Traceback|Error|FAILED|assert|OOM|Killed` → **no hits**, not zero-gated. Plus the
`PRODUCTION_DONE job=671` breadcrumb.

**The numbers.** `world_size = 8`, `n_steps = 1290` — which is exactly the independent prediction for
ws8 × bs8 × 10 epochs over 8,303 records ((8303//8)//8 × 10 = 1,290), so the step budget is confirmed
from two directions rather than one. Fold scope: `n_records = 8303`, `n_training_fold_records = 8303`,
`selection_val_excluded = false`, `n_selection_val_excluded = 0`, `fold_scope = "train"` (not
`inner_train`) — the **full ADR-0004 D5 fold**, user decision (1). Peak VRAM **1.0948 GiB/rank**,
matching the P2-05 sizing measurement to four decimals. Checkpoint sha256 `5e9939c3…` verified
**locally after rsync against the hash the run itself recorded**.

**No val metric, by construction.** `eval_requested = false`, `eval_metrics = null`,
`eval_scope = null`, `gate4_graded = false`. imp.md's "held-out val F1 reported" gate is **met by
refusal** (user decision (1)): `selection_val` is now inside the training set and the LOO holdout is
P2-14's grading set, so no free rung exists. The `eval_val_scored_on_disjoint_fold` clause is
vacuously true here — but *grounded* vacuity: `eval_requested = false` is recorded evidence that
nothing was scored, not an absent-evidence default ([[clauses-must-guard-emptiness]]).

**Two honest caveats.** (i) `provenance.git_dirty = true`. Investigated rather than waved through:
`git diff --stat` over tracked non-`data` paths is **empty** — the dirt is exactly the staged
`split_assignments.parquet` (real content vs the committed LFS pointer, hash-verified) plus the run's
own untracked outputs. **Zero tracked code differs**, so `git_sha 0f8d527` does describe what ran.
(ii) The report's top-level `step` field reads `"P2-04"` — the entrypoint's own label, inherited; the
checkpoint sidecar correctly carries `step: "P2-09"`. Cosmetic, recorded here rather than silently
reconciled.

**Decisions.** Round-0 is **not** gate-graded — GATE-4 is P2-14 on the leave-one-order-out holdout.
Mining is P2-10. **Commit.** SHA pending.

**Review (CodeRabbit, run commit) — 1 rejected, 1 accepted.** *Rejected:*
`eval_val_scored_on_disjoint_fold: true` was flagged as "skipped evaluation represented as
successful validation". It is not: `_eval_val_ok`'s not-requested branch returns
`requested is False and metrics_block is None and scope is None` — a **positive three-part
coherence assertion**, which returns *False* for a run that skipped eval yet still carried metrics.
Setting it false as suggested would fail `overall_pass` on every correct production run. The finding
read the JSON, not the clause. *Accepted:* `provenance_complete: true` beside `git_dirty: true` was a
real gap — `_provenance_complete` ignored `git_dirty` entirely, so a run with genuinely modified
**code** would have certified identically, its `git_sha` naming a commit that does not contain what
ran. Requiring a clean tree would fail every cluster run (no git-lfs there ⇒ re-staging dirties the
tree by construction), so the fix **classifies** instead of prohibiting: `_git_dirty_paths()` records
the porcelain path list, and the clause requires every dirty path to be data-staging, failing closed
when a dirty tree carries no path list. Sabotage-verified — reverting to the old always-true form
fails exactly `test_provenance_clause_separates_staged_data_dirt_from_dirty_code` and
`test_committed_production_report_records_its_audited_dirty_tree`.

**The checkpoint was NOT re-run** (user decision 2026-07-20): ADR-0002 A7 gives metric-level, not
bitwise, determinism, so regenerating the report purely to satisfy the new clause would ship
*different weights* for no scientific gain. Consequence, stated plainly rather than buried: job 671's
committed report predates the `git_dirty_paths` field, so the strengthened clause **re-derives
`provenance_complete: False` against its stored `True`**. The stored claim is nonetheless *correct* —
audited at retrieval on the cluster checkout at `0f8d527`, where `git status --porcelain` showed only
` M data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet` plus untracked outputs and `git diff --stat`
over tracked non-`data` paths was **empty**. Following the P2-06 precedent (`test_sweep_selection.py`
locked the 36 points' audited flags), that manual audit is now itself a test: it pins both the stored
`True` and the re-derived `False`, and fails loudly if the report is ever regenerated — at which
point the paths exist and the lock must be replaced by the automatic check. Every run from P2-10 on
records `git_dirty_paths` and is verified without a human in the loop. **Validation.** CI-exact, all
gates armed: **1,787 passed / 162 skipped** (+3, zero regressions); ruff + black clean.

**Review (follow-up round, 3 findings — all applied).** Two majors on the provenance fix itself,
both real. (1) `_git_dirty()` and `_git_dirty_paths()` each shelled out to `git status`
independently, so the boolean and the path list could describe **two different working trees** — and
the classification would then be judging evidence that never co-existed. Now one
`_git_status_snapshot()` is read in `build_report` and passed to both. (2) The line-based parser
split on a literal `" -> "` to strip rename origins, but a **filename may legally contain that
string** — it would report a phantom path and could even rewrite a dirty source file into a
`data/`-prefixed one and thereby certify provenance. Now `--porcelain=v1 -z`, parsed structurally:
NUL-separated `XY <path>` records, with a rename's extra origin field consumed and discarded.
(3, minor) `git_sha` was checked as a 40-character string; now `[0-9a-f]{40}`. Verified against the
**real** tree, not only the synthetic fixture: the parser reproduces `git status --porcelain`'s own
output on this worktree and correctly classifies `src/…` and `tests/…` as code, so a run made right
now would rightly refuse to certify. **Validation.** CI-exact, all gates armed: **1,788 passed / 162
skipped**, zero regressions; ruff + black clean.

**Review (final round, 1 major — applied, and the sharpest of the step).**
`test_build_report_reads_the_working_tree_only_once` was **tautological**: it called
`_git_status_snapshot()` directly and asserted one call — proving only that calling a function once
calls it once. It never drove `build_report`, the thing the invariant is about. Demonstrated rather
than argued: re-applying the double-read defect to `build_report` and running the OLD test **passes**
(`1 passed`), while the rewritten test — which now drives the real `build_report` and asserts both
that exactly one `status` call occurs and that the two derived fields agree — **fails**. A test named
for an invariant it does not exercise is the same failure as [[tests-can-specify-the-bug]], and it
survived my own sabotage discipline because I sabotaged the *implementation* and watched a test that
was never wired to it. Sabotaging the code proves a test bites; it does not prove the test is
connected to the code. **Validation.** CI-exact, all gates armed: **1,788 passed / 162 skipped**,
zero regressions; ruff + black clean.

## Step P2-10a — 2026-07-20: the Stage-1 scanner — checkpoint loader + pad-aware scan, and the P2-10 split that preceded it

**Inputs.** `data/processed/checkpoints/stage1_production/stage1.pt` (P2-09, sha256 `5e9939c3…`, a bare
`state_dict` with **no architecture metadata**) · `infer/reconcile.py::reconcile_windows` (the pinned
ADR-0005 D3 + A3 operator) · `window_dataset::{encode_bases,tile_windows,carve_window,PAD_TOKEN_ID}` ·
`conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml` (`rc_combine=concat`).

**Process.** P2-10 as written was **unexecutable**, and this step opened by measuring that rather than
working around it. `mining_round_readiness` was executed directly: it returns `ready=false` for an
all-`False` backend set (*"no model-independent disjunct backend is available; every candidate would be
spared"*) **and** for a `(b)`-only set (*"relaxed_architecture is False on every Tier-2N locus (ADR-0006
D9 row 5)"*). P2-07's own stanza had already recorded that the gate "refuses every round **by design
until P6**" — so P2-10 was sequenced ahead of the P6-01 step supplying its critical-path backend. Five
further gaps were measured, not inferred: **zero** `torch.load`/`load_state_dict` in `src/` (no scanner
existed at all); `decoys_v0.parquet`'s `accession`/`locus_start`/`locus_end` are Arrow type `null`, **0
non-null of 7,008**; `rscape` absent from every `envs/*.yml` and lockfile; no negative-injection or
warm-start hook in `train_stage1`; and **N, the round count, pinned nowhere** — imp.md's *"round budget
frozen at the phase gate"* had imported **D3's** phrasing, D14 pins no number, and `"mining"` appears
**0×** in ADR-0003, so A2's ≤ 90 GPU-h bound never covered mining retrains. User decision (2026-07-20):
**split and build in place** → P2-10a…e. This stanza is P2-10a.

`src/tbox_finder/infer/scan.py` lands `encode_scan_window` (pad-aware, `PAD_TOKEN_ID`), `scan_window_ids`,
`scan_encoded_windows`, `scan_sequence`, `load_stage1_checkpoint`, `checkpoint_key_report`.

**Outputs.** `infer/scan.py` · `train_stage1::evaluate_selection_val` delegating · `tests/unit/test_infer_scan.py`
(18) · `tests/ml/test_scan_checkpoint.py` (14).

**Validation.** **PASS, all four gates.** (1) The committed checkpoint fits the rebuilt segmenter:
**309 keys, 0 missing / 0 unexpected / 0 shape-mismatch**, `fits=true` — and *both* negative controls
bite with concrete evidence (`rc_combine=gate` → `head.classifier.weight: checkpoint(8, 512) vs
model(8, 256)`; `use_crf=True` → 3 missing CRF params). (2) The analytic closed form matches at every
probed position. (3) Zero-flank geometry holds at both ends of the range. (4) The delegation is
behaviour-preserving: **69 passed** with `TBOX_REQUIRE_TRAIN_CUDA=1` on real CUDA + the real corpus.
Like-for-like suite (clean worktree at `082f8c7`, no DVC data, same CI-exact command): **1,773 → 1,791
passed, 168 → 182 skipped** = **+18 / +14, exactly the additions, zero regressions**. ruff + black clean.

**Decisions.**

**The seam is the loop, not the encoder.** The trainer's tile→forward→reconcile loop was the *only* one
in the repo, and its own docstring is why it must not be forked: evaluating through the deployed
operator is what makes "a config selected here is selected under the arithmetic it will be deployed
under" true, so a second copy in the scanner would let the config *selected* drift from the arithmetic
*deployed* — precisely the failure that docstring exists to prevent. But the two callers must **not**
share an encoder: `encode_eval_window` *raises* on an overrun, and that guard is real (a boundary
invented mid-context is a falsehood, not a contig end), whereas a scanner is handed Rfam decoys of
~100–200 nt against a 1024-nt window whose ends genuinely *are* ends. Collapsing them into one
permissive encoder would have deleted a live guard under cover of de-duplication. So: one loop, two
encoding policies. Mode handling restores the **observed** state rather than a hardcoded one, which is
what makes the per-record call safe to nest inside the trainer's outer `eval()`.

**Shapes are compared, not just key names — and my first justification for that was false.** I wrote
that `concat` and `gate` "produce the same key names, so only the head's input width differs", making a
key-set check vacuously green for the wrong mode. That is **not true**: `RCCombine` registers
`gate_logit` only in `gate` mode, so the key *sets* differ — and my own negative-control run had already
printed `missing: rc_combine.gate_logit` on screen while I was writing the opposite. Caught by the
adversarial review (three independent lenses, same defect). The shape comparison is still right, for a
different and true reason: the mismatches key names **cannot** catch are the ones that keep the same
parameter set and change only its extent — a different-`d_model` backbone, or a different class count.
Those differ solely in shape and would otherwise load as a clean 0/0 fit. The test now pins the
**measured** counts (`gate` → `head_missing == ['rc_combine.gate_logit']` *and* `n_shape_mismatch == 1`;
`use_crf=True` → 3 missing `head.crf.*`, 0 reshaped) rather than an assumed story. The loader runs at
`strict=False` plus an
explicit non-empty check: per context7 (`/pytorch/pytorch`, serialization notes) `strict=False` **still
raises on shape mismatch**, so this is the same hard failure as `strict=True` *plus* the key lists that
make a mismatch diagnosable. `weights_only=True` is the torch ≥ 2.6 default and correct for a pure
`state_dict`.

**My own test docstring over-claimed, and sabotage is what caught it.** The closed-form test claimed to
bite on "a pad leak". Sabotage S1 (pad with the `N` id instead of `PAD_TOKEN_ID`) failed three *other*
tests and left **every parametrisation of the closed form green** — because `scan_window_ids` only ever
pads out-of-bounds positions and `reconcile_windows` slices those away before any arithmetic. The claim
was false. It also exposed a real gap: the unit tier has no torch, so it calls `reconcile_windows`
*directly* and never exercises `scan_sequence`'s own wiring — above all the `seq_len` it passes, the one
place a pad genuinely could enter. The docstring was corrected to state what is measured, and
`tests/ml/test_scan_checkpoint.py` was added to drive `scan_sequence` end-to-end with a CPU stub. This is
the P2-05 lesson recurring one level up: a false blanket claim in one's own test docstring.

**Sabotage log — five on the implementation, one on the delegation, each attributed by name.**
S1 pad-with-`N` → 3 fail; S2 left-overrun padded on the wrong side → `test_negative_start_pads_on_the_left_not_the_right`;
S3 all-pad-window refusal removed → `test_window_covering_no_real_position_is_refused`;
S4 overlap removed from the tiling → `test_window_ids_match_tile_windows_offsets`;
S5 `starts` off by one → 9 fail incl. 3 of 4 closed-form parametrisations. Separately, reversing the
`starts` order *inside the promoted loop* turned the **trainer's** `test_the_val_eval_scores_a_perfect_oracle_at_f1_one`
and `test_a_one_nucleotide_shift_destroys_the_oracle_score` red — which is the evidence that the
delegation is genuinely live in the trainer's path and not merely importable. Tree restored and verified
by sha256 after every mutation (the stale-`__pycache__` precedent: `__pycache__` cleared and
`-p no:cacheprovider` on every run).

**The adversarial review found two defects my own sabotage discipline did not.** Five read-only lenses ×
independent refutation verifiers: 32 candidate findings, **26 refuted, 6 confirmed** — which collapse to
**two** distinct defects, each caught by three lenses independently. (1) The `rc_combine` justification
above. (2) `test_load_refuses_a_checkpoint_that_does_not_fit` was **over-determined**: passing
`backbone=object()` leaves the rebuilt model with no `backbone.*` parameters, so all **307** backbone
keys in the real checkpoint land in `unexpected_keys` and the loader raises *regardless* of head
geometry — making the `use_crf=True` argument inert and leaving the `missing` half of the guard, plus
the loader's entire **positive** path, untested. Both are the same shape of error as S8 in P2-07: a test
that passes for a reason other than the one it is named for. Fixed by stripping the `backbone.*` keys
into a head-only temp checkpoint so each branch fails on its own merits, and by adding the missing
positive-path test (loads cleanly, comes back in `eval()`, and the *trained* weights are asserted equal
to the file's — not a freshly initialised head). Verified: dropping the `missing` term from the guard
now fails `test_load_refuses_each_wrong_geometry_on_its_own_merits[kwargs0-missing]`, the exact
regression the old test would have shipped green. Tree audited after the review (`git diff` empty) — the
lenses were read-only, per the standing hazard that review agents can mutate the code under review.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** This is the transport only. Along-sequence locus construction (threshold,
minimum span, gap-merge — the rest of ADR-0005 D3) is **not** here; `scan_sequence` stops at the
per-position distribution. No mining round is closer to runnable than it was: P2-10b/c/d/e remain, and
**P2-10c carries an env-amendment stop and P2-10e an ADR stop for the still-unpinned N**. The tier-2
checkpoint gate is armed by the artifact's *presence*, so it skips in CI — it was run locally against the
real `stage1.pt` (sha256 verified against the P2-09 provenance) and its values are recorded above.

**Commit.** `<SHA — reconcile against `git log main` at the P2 exit gate>`

**CodeRabbit r1 — 1 finding, applied.** `scan_encoded_windows` left an empty `window_ids` to
`torch.cat([])`, which raises "expected a non-empty list of Tensors" — an error naming neither the
caller's mistake nor this module. Unreachable through `scan_sequence` (`tile_windows` always emits ≥ 1
window) but the function is public and the trainer calls it directly, so it is now a named refusal with
a test that bites under sabotage.

## Step P2-10b — 2026-07-20: a coordinate-bearing mineable negative pool

**Inputs.** `data/processed/negatives/decoys_v0.parquet` (7,008 records, four §9.1 pools);
`data/external/refs/decoys/{structured_rna_refs.fa,leader_decoys.fa}`; `data/interim/flank_context/context_v0.parquet`
(23,535 rows, 23,532 anchored, P2-00); `data/processed/priors/union_prior.parquet` (24,160 rows);
`masking.LocusIndex`; `mining/hard_negative.classify_candidate`.

**Process.** ADR-0005 D14 makes the union-prior locus mask the first guard between a Stage-1 false
positive and the mined pool, and it could not run: `classify_candidate` returned
`refused_no_coordinates` for **100 %** of every pool in `MINEABLE_POOLS`. Four measurements decided the
shape of the fix, all re-derived independently before any code was written. (1) The Rfam headers do
carry coordinates — **3,000/3,000** parse as `<accession>/<start>-<end>` — but **0 of 2,750** distinct (of the 2,999 coordinate-bearing records; 2,751 across all 3,000 headers)
accessions intersect the `LocusIndex` keys, because every union-prior key is unversioned (**0 of
12,041**) and 2,999/3,000 Rfam accessions are versioned; version-stripping recovers **269**. (2) Even
then, `structured_rna` contains **0 true overlaps** with the union prior — the nearest record is 5 nt
away and only the 50-nt flank catches it, so the pool cannot demonstrate the mask fires. (3)
`dinuc_shuffled`'s only possible coordinates are its source positives'. (4) `leader_decoy` (N = 8) has
opaque surrogate ids with no recoverable accession. So the step parsed the Rfam coordinates
(`decoys.parse_structured_rna_locus`), pinned namespace normalisation at the shared masking boundary
(`masking.normalize_accession`, both index build and query), and built the substrate none of the four
pools could be — `mining/pool.py`, real windows carved from the `flank_context` regions with exact
`(accession, locus_start, locus_end, strand)` on replicons that host known T-boxes.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/mining/pool.py`; `masking.{normalize_accession, accession_namespace_report,
is_missing}` + a version-normalising `LocusIndex`; `data/flank_context.forward_bounds`;
`decoys.parse_structured_rna_locus` + `strand`/`source_record_id` columns;
`rule build_mining_pool` (`workflow/rules/data.smk`); `data/processed/negatives/mining_pool_v0.parquet`
(46,591 windows, DVC `84319675…`); `data/processed/audits/mining_pool_report.json`; rebuilt
`decoys_v0.parquet` (DVC `be562333…`, was `da657ba0…`).

**Validation.** Gate PASS. Designed control **500/500** masked (500/500 true overlaps at flank 0);
natural **85/46,091** = 0.184 % (69 true overlaps); namespace **0 as-is → 11,447 normalised**;
`structured_rna` coordinate coverage **2,999/3,000**; `refused_no_coordinates` **0/2,000** sampled
substrate records. Suite **1,848 passed / 184 skipped** (the two new files collect **61** tests; the +56 delta quoted here is superseded — see the follow-up stanza) against a like-for-like `main` baseline
(same data symlinks, clean worktree at `35b62be`) of **1,792 / 184** = **+56, zero regressions**;
ruff + black + `snakemake --lint` + `snakemake -n` clean. The decoy golden digest is **unchanged**
(`13061e3e…`) — the schema change is pure column addition.

**The measurement that changed the design.** The gate the step inherited — "a known-locus-overlapping
candidate is actually masked" — could not be met by any natural pool, because the natural overlap count
was zero. A natural rate of zero is a legitimate biological result, so it can never distinguish
"nothing overlaps" from "the mask is structurally dead", and a gate resting on it is unfalsifiable in
the wrong direction. The gate is therefore carried by a **designed** control: locus-centred windows,
which overlap a known T-box by construction and must mask at **100 %**. That control immediately earned
its keep. It scored **23,147/23,535** — and the shortfall was not biology. `context_seq` is
reverse-complemented server-side for minus-strand records (this module's own `revcomp` is never called
on that path), so index 0 is the *highest* forward coordinate; and `region_stop` is the *requested*
stop, which `plan_region` cannot clamp because the contig length is unknown before the fetch, so every
clipped region is skewed. Over the 23,532 `status == "ok"` rows: anchoring to `region_start` scored
**23,146**, anchoring to `region_stop` scored **23,187** (all 345 residual misses `clipped_start`), and
anchoring to `region_start + len(context_seq) - 1` — the actual last fetched base — scores
**23,532/23,532**. The corrected frame also revised the measured contamination **down**: on the
lead-flank windows alone (the pilot measurement, 23,040 windows) from **0.84 % to 0.26 %**, the first
number being inflated by the frame error. *(The shipped rate quoted above, **0.184 %**, is a different
and larger denominator — lead **and** trail, 85/46,091 — not a third value for the same quantity.)*
A control that had merely "looked high" would have shipped the bug and a wrong contamination rate with it.

**Decisions.** Two §7 stops were taken to the user and both resolved as recommended.
(i) The pre-declared Q-question — `dinuc_shuffled` is **not** mineable; carrying its parents' coordinates
would mask 100 % of the pool against itself and turn the non-vacuity number into a tautology, so it
leaves `MINEABLE_POOLS` (ADR-0005 **A6**, signed 2026-07-20) and keeps its §9.1 training role, with the
parent link now recorded as a content-addressed `source_record_id` instead of a corpus row index baked
into `decoy_id` text. (ii) A **new** stop, raised by the measurements: re-sourcing a true
5′UTR/tRNA-adjacent `leader_decoy` pool needs CDS/tRNA coordinates the repo does not have (zero GFF/GBK,
both NCBI fetchers `rettype=fasta`, no `tRNAscan-SE`/`hmmer`/aaRS resource — 3 acquisitions + 2 env
amendments) and is **not P2-sized**; it is deferred to **P2-10b′** and said so rather than declaring the
pool "re-sourced" because it grew. The namespace fix was pinned at the shared boundary rather than in
each producer so a future pool cannot silently re-introduce the empty-intersection no-op; this is a
semantics change to a key ADR-0006 D11 pins, recorded in A6's impact list with the D11 amendment
batched to the P2 exit gate by user decision.

**Sabotage verification.** Four reverts, `__pycache__` cleared and `-p no:cacheprovider` each time, each
checked by `^FAILED` name rather than a red count: identity `normalize_accession` → 11 failed incl.
`test_a_versioned_query_masks_against_an_unversioned_index` and the ml namespace gate; forward-only
minus-strand frame → 4 failed incl. `test_every_locus_centred_control_window_masks`; `has_coordinates`
back to `is not None` → the 3 NaN tests (that guard was a live fail-open: a pandas round-trip through a
nullable column yields NaN, which passes an identity check and is then classified **mined**, not
refused); unguarded gate clauses → the 6 emptiness tests. Restore verified green (56 passed) with a
clean `git status`.

**Disclosed non-delivery.** `refused_no_coordinates` is **not** zero everywhere: it remains 100 % for
`gc_background` (impossible in principle), `dinuc_shuffled` (by A6), and `leader_decoy` (pending
P2-10b′), and 1/3,000 for `structured_rna` — an RNAcentral URS with an interval but no replicon, refused
rather than given a coordinate that addresses no genome. The `genomic_window` substrate is
annotation-blind: its windows are **not** verified 5′UTRs and nothing here may be described as a leader
decoy. ADR-0006 D12's per-class joint false-pass estimate still rests on the 8 leader records and is
under-powered until P2-10b′. No mining round is runnable yet — P2-10c (env stop) and P2-10e (the ADR
stop for the still-unpinned round count N) remain.

**Pre-commit adversarial review (5 read-only lenses, 49 raised / 9 confirmed).** Ten fixes landed
before the commit; the tree was audited afterwards (`git status` shows only the intended paths, and the
one `SABOTAGE` string in the tree is a pre-existing committed comment in `test_reconcile.py` from P2-03).
The findings worth recording: (1) **three of the new tests were tautologies.**
`test_normalisation_is_what_makes_the_namespaces_compatible` compared `raw_accession_keys` against
`accession_keys` — but on this data those sets are *identical* (no union-prior accession carries a
version), so the assertion was invariant to the function it was named for and **stayed green under the
exact sabotage it advertised**; it now asserts against `normalize_accession` directly and does bite.
The decoy-overlap test asserted `n_overlapping <= n_masked`, a monotonicity identity no input can
falsify, where the claim that matters is the *value* (`== 0`, the premise ADR-0005 A6 rests on). The
partition test restated how both sides were derived; it now asserts the two partitions are disjoint and
exhaust the pool. (2) **The gate was one notch too loose:** it required controls to be *masked* (flank
50) when the stricter *overlap* count (flank 0) was already computed and sitting unread in the same
