dict — a uniform frame error below 50 nt would have scored a perfect 100 %. It now gates on
`all_controls_overlap` too. (3) `_REPLICON_ACCESSION_RE` rejected `NZ_`-prefixed RefSeq accessions
(letters after the underscore) — **823 of this project's own 12,041 union-prior accessions**. Harmless
today (the staged Rfam refs carry only `NC_`/`NW_`), a live trap on any resubsample. (4) A gate-**failed**
substrate was written to the canonical path before the raise, where `dvc add` would not have consulted
the gate; the parquet and its provenance are now written only after the gate passes, while the report —
the diagnosis — is still written unconditionally. (5) The `refused_no_coordinates` test coerced
`str(row.accession)`, which would have laundered a null into the literal `"nan"` and passed. Also
corrected: three quoted denominators (the frame scores are **23,146** / **23,187** / **23,532** over the
**23,532** `status == "ok"` rows, not /23,535), and the dev-log's two contamination rates, which were
correct but read as contradictory. Artifact md5s are unchanged by all ten fixes.

**Commit.** `<SHA — reconcile against `git log main` at the P2 exit gate>`

**CodeRabbit r1 — 3 nitpicks, all applied.** All three were the same species as the pre-commit
findings: a predicate stated twice and free to drift. (1) `coordinate_coverage.n_with_coordinates`
keyed off the accession alone while the masking loop required all three fields — consistent today only
because `parse_structured_rna_locus` is all-or-nothing, so a future producer setting an accession
without coordinates would have over-counted coverage *and* fed unmaskable accessions to the namespace
report; both now route through one `_has_coordinates`. (2) `build` recomputed `index.is_masked` per
record to populate the artifact's `masked` column, duplicating the call `mask_pool` had already made
for the reported counts — the artifact's own column could have drifted from the report that grades it;
both now go through `window_is_masked`. (3) `mining_pool_report.json` recorded `context_sha256` and
`union_prior_sha256` but not the corpus, which supplies `own_loci` and therefore determines every
masked/overlap count in the report; `corpus_sha256` added. No artifact md5 changed (`84319675…`,
`be562333…`) and the decoy golden digest is still `13061e3e…`; suite re-run **1,853 / 184**.

## Step P2-10b (follow-up) — 2026-07-20: hardening the guards the review found decorative

**Why.** The P2-10b review's first pass raised 49 findings but 26 of its verifier agents died on
rate limits, so ~40 findings were recorded "refuted" on no evidence. A second, leaner pass (14 batched
verifiers, zero failures, every verdict backed by executed sabotage) re-graded those 40: **17 REAL**,
13 already fixed by the CodeRabbit round, 10 genuinely not real. An independent adjudicator confirmed
all of them. **Nothing shipped a wrong number** — every finding was a guard weaker than advertised —
but one landed on the step's own headline.

**The headline guard did not test what it claimed.** The stanza above says the 100 %-must-mask control
"proves the mask is live". It proves less than that. Every locus-centred control is carved from a
`flank_context` row that *is* a corpus positive, so it masks against `own_loci` whether or not the
union prior is alive: sabotaging `load_union_loci` to return `[]` left **all 61 tests green with zero
new `FAILED` lines**, and masking from union-only, own-only, and both gives *bit-identical* counts
(control 500/500, natural 85/46,091). The report would still have printed `union_denominator: 24160`,
because that figure is derived from the parquet's row count, not from the loci actually loaded. ADR-0005
D14's first guard could have degraded silently to own-positives-only. `build()` now refuses when the
prior reports maskable records but yields none, the report carries `n_union_loci_loaded` /
`n_own_positive_loci_loaded` beside the denominator, and a test asserts the **414** accessions only the
union prior contributes are addressable in a union-only index.

**Overlap was the wrong strictness for a frame check.** A control window *is* the locus, so overlap
survives a shift of up to the locus length: measured uniform shifts through **±160 nt** kept 500/500
controls masked *and* overlapping while the reported natural contamination moved 0.184 % → 0.473 %.
Exact reproduction of the known interval has zero tolerance — and it holds, **23,532/23,532**, with a
±1 nt shift taking it to **0**. So `all_controls_exact` is now the binding clause and *every* control
discriminates a frame error, rather than the ~1.6 % of rows a shift happens to push clear of the locus
(only **5** of the shipped 500). One measurement forced the design: matching against the *merged*
spans scores 22,763/23,532, because merging is lossy and 769 windows sit on accessions whose loci
merged — so exact identity is tested against the retained **pre-merge source** intervals. Fitting a
threshold to 96.7 % would have been the §10.3 failure.

**Other confirmed guards, each now sabotage-verified.** `n_controls` was a *request*, not a contract
(8 requested → 2 carved → gate green, shortfall recorded nowhere) → `all_requested_controls_carved`.
`test_an_unanchored_row_yields_no_window` tripped two guards at once and stayed green under removal of
either alone — the fixture now isolates each. The "determinism" test compared two same-seed calls, so
it passed with the seed ignored entirely → a seed-42-vs-43 assertion. `source_record_id` and its
content-addressed corpus join had no test on either leg, and re-narrowing the corpus read silently
re-points all 2,000 links while leaving them non-null. `carve_window` stringified the accession, so a
null would have become `"nan"` and classified *minable*. `margin_nt >= flank_nt` was load-bearing with
0 nt of slack and written down nowhere (flank 51 adds 3 self-masked windows). And
`TBOX_REQUIRE_MINING_POOL` was armed **nowhere** — 6 of 9 gate clauses skipped green in CI by default;
the committed-JSON clauses now *raise* on a missing file (stronger than a var, and it cannot rot into
an unarmed one) while the parquet tier stays an explicit §8.5 step-local gate, documented in `ci.yml`.

**Validation.** Gate PASS with 7 clauses: control **500/500 exact / 500/500 overlapping / 500/500
masked**, requested == carved (500), natural 85/46,091, namespace compatible. Suite **1,880 passed /
184 skipped** (from 1,873/164 on the merged step; +7 armed by the new data-present clauses). Decoy
golden digest unchanged (`13061e3e…`); both artifact md5s unchanged (`84319675…`, `be562333…`).
Seven sabotage reverts each bite the test **named** for the invariant, `^FAILED` grepped every time.

**Corrections.** `pool.py`'s stale `23,147/23,535` → `23,146/23,532`; `0 of 2,751` → `0 of 2,750`
(the coordinate-bearing denominator, disambiguated); the namespace report now publishes
`n_distinct_normalized` / `n_unaddressable_normalized` so a version collapse (6 groups) no longer reads
as a coverage shortfall.

**Still open, recorded not hidden.** `controls_present` remains strictly implied by the other control
clauses — kept with a test rather than deleted. The dev-log's earlier `+56` should have been `+61`
(the two new files collect 61 tests); absolute suite counts are env-dependent and only the delta is
meaningful. The union-prior liveness check asserts the prior *contributes*, not that any specific
masking outcome depends on it — measured, no shipped number does.

**CodeRabbit r1 (CLI rung — the GitHub route was rate-limited) — 1 Major, applied.** The new
committed-file guard used `all(p in _TRACKED for p in missing)`, so a **mixed** missing set fell
through to the skip branch: `_need(_POOL, _POOL_REPORT)` passes exactly such a set, so a checkout that
had lost its committed report *and* its DVC parquet would have skipped green rather than raising —
the same fail-open shape this change exists to remove, reintroduced by the fix for it. Now any missing
tracked file raises regardless of what else is absent; verified to raise on a mixed set.

**Commit.** `<SHA — reconcile against `git log main` at the P2 exit gate>`

## Step P2-10c — 2026-07-20: the protective spare-rule backend — (a) any-helix R-scape covariation

**Inputs.** `envs/infernal.yml` (the `infernal` 1.1.5 pin and its GATE-1-load-bearing lockfile),
`src/tbox_finder/mining/spare_rule.py` (`mining_round_readiness`, `SpareRuleEvidence`),
`src/tbox_finder/mining/hard_negative.py::mine_round`, `data/interim/splits/aligned/class_II.sto`
(the fixture source), and bioconda.

**Process.** Pinned `rscape` **2.0.4.a** into a **new 7th env** `envs/rscape.yml`
(+ `envs/rscape.conda-lock.yml`, 125 packages, conda-lock 4.0.2) rather than amending
`envs/infernal.yml`, and wrote `src/tbox_finder/mining/covariation.py` — a `shutil.which` availability
probe, a version assertion, a `subprocess` R-scape driver, and a pure-stdlib `.helixcov` parser that
turns helix-level covariation into the `passed`/`failed` status `SpareRuleEvidence` expects.
`round_backend_availability` constructs the `mine_round` availability map with the `any_helix_rscape`
entry **derived from the probe and deliberately un-overridable**. `scripts/make_rscape_fixture.py`
regenerates the committed matched-control fixture pair.

**Outputs.** `envs/rscape.yml` + `envs/rscape.conda-lock.yml`
(`content_hash.linux-64 = cf43795d8d3ddf7cdf2471e38a7b2a790e58a85a126c21fcb3d42e1ecc60d917`);
`src/tbox_finder/mining/covariation.py`; `scripts/make_rscape_fixture.py`;
`tests/fixtures/rscape/` (4 files, byte-reproducible from the script);
`tests/unit/test_covariation.py` (22) + `tests/ml/test_rscape_backend.py` (8, armed by
`TBOX_REQUIRE_RSCAPE=1`); `"rscape"` added to `EXPECTED_ENVS`. No DVC artifact, no Snakemake rule,
no SLURM.

**Validation.** **The step's stated gate was a tautology and was replaced, not weakened.**
`mining_round_readiness({'any_helix_rscape': True, …})` returns `ready=true` on `main` today with zero
code changes — availability is a caller-supplied `bool`, so the clause is satisfied by typing `True`.
Worse, flipping it on a backend that cannot score does not merely fail to help: every candidate then
carries `unavailable` evidence, `is_mining_excluded` spares on any `unavailable`, and the round emits a
report of zeroes indistinguishable from "there was nothing to mine" — the exact failure `mine_round`'s
docstring says the readiness gate exists to prevent. The gate is therefore carried by a **designed
matched control**: a seeded 60-sequence subsample of the class-II alignment versus the *same*
subsample with every column independently permuted across rows. Held fixed — the sequences, each
column's exact base-and-gap composition, `#=GC SS_cons`, the alignment width, `E ≤ 0.05`, the binary.
Varied — only the correlation *between* columns. **PASS: positive 8 helices / max 4 covarying pairs in
one helix / 13 total; control the same 8 helices with identical coordinates and base-pair counts and
0 covarying pairs everywhere.** Suite **1,945 passed / 158 skipped** with the binary present, against a
like-for-like `main` baseline of **1,915 / 158** — **+30, zero regressions**. Three sabotage reverts
each bit the test *named* for the invariant (`^FAILED` grepped): criterion-always-true killed
`test_designed_control_separates_under_every_criterion[within_helix]`; **availability-hardcoded-`True`
— the precise tautology this step exists to remove — killed
`test_readiness_refuses_when_the_binary_is_absent` and
`test_availability_cannot_be_forced_by_an_argument`**; a dead parser killed both criteria's control
tests. The armed gate also caught a real bug during development: `_VERSION_RE` matched the banner's
title line and reported the version as `::`.

**Decisions.** ADR-0002 **A11** (signed 2026-07-20) records the env. A **separate** env because
`rscape` pulls `gnuplot`'s Qt5/GStreamer closure: amending `envs/infernal.yml` would take it 39 → 135
packages and force a re-solve of the lockfile the **GATE-1 recall claim** is measured against, for no
saving (the standalone env is 125 packages either way). `infernal` 1.1.5 is pinned in the new env at
the **same build string** (`pl5321h7b50bb2_4`); the feared `esl-afetch`/`esl-reformat` collision was
settled by a **real co-install** (exit 0; both toolchains report their pinned versions afterwards), not
by a solver dry-run. Version **2.0.4.a is the newest build bioconda ships** while upstream is
**v2.6.11** — approved **for the mining protective filter only**, because v2.0.5 recalculated the
covariation power curves, which makes the version a methods-section fact; ADR-0006 D2's *reported* (a)
criterion at GATE-3/P6 must re-decide it (D8 bump trigger).

**Left open, recorded not hidden.** Two things.
(1) **The env + caller are necessary but not sufficient.** R-scape scores an *alignment*, never a
single sequence, and ADR-0006 D2→D7 require the model-independent one. The repo has **no
homolog-search target database** (`data/external` is 81 MB of T-box-specific FASTA; no RefSeq/GTDB/nt)
and **no `hmmer`/`blast` in any env**, so no per-candidate verdict is producible yet. That is
**P2-10c′**, sequenced with P6-01, and it is why the availability probe has no override argument.
(2) **"any-helix" admits two operators and neither is pinned.** `WITHIN_HELIX` (≥2 covarying pairs
inside one helix) and `TOTAL_ACROSS_HELICES` (≥2 summed across helices) are not equivalent. Measured on
seeded subsamples of the class-II alignment they agree on high-power inputs (0 % divergence at 5–8 and
60 sequences) and **disagree on up to 33 %** of low-power ones (10/12/15/20/30 sequences: 17 %, 25 %,
17 %, 33 %, 8 %) — the regime a real candidate's homolog set occupies. Every divergent case is one the
looser operator spares and the stricter one sends to the mining pool, so the choice is directly a
Tier-2N-protection choice. `AnyHelixCriterion` therefore has **no default** and `covariation_verdict`
requires it positionally; `CovariationResult.as_report` records the counterfactual verdict. Pinning one
is an **ADR-0006 decision owed at P2-10e**.

**Commit.** `<SHA — reconcile against `git log main` at the P2 exit gate>`

**CodeRabbit r1 — 3 Major + 2 nits, all applied.** The Major that mattered undercut A11's own
narrative: **the pinned version was documented everywhere and asserted nowhere it was used.**
`backend_available()` checked only that *some* `R-scape` was on `PATH`, and `as_report()` stamped
`PINNED_RSCAPE_VERSION` **unconditionally** — so a 2.6.x binary could have produced the numbers while
every committed report claimed 2.0.4.a, which is precisely the field this step argued a reader most
needs to be true. Now: availability is presence **and** version (a mismatch reads *unavailable*, so
the readiness gate refuses the round — the fail-closed direction); `CovariationResult` carries the
**observed** version (`None` for a parse, which is no evidence of what ran) and the report publishes
`rscape_version_observed` / `_pinned` / `_matches_pin`; `run_rscape` refuses an unpinned build outright;
and `scripts/make_rscape_fixture.py` checks the pin **before** overwriting tracked fixtures. Second
Major: the fixture generator wrote incrementally, so a failure on the second R-scape run would leave a
fresh positive beside a stale control — a **mismatched pair that still looks matched**, the one thing
the gate cannot tolerate; all four files are now staged in a temp dir and moved only after both runs
succeed. Third: the reproduction test claimed "byte-for-byte" while comparing only parsed helices, so a
changed p-value or aggregation mode would have slipped through — it now diffs the raw file. Two
sabotage reverts confirm the new invariants bite by name
(`test_an_unpinned_build_reads_as_unavailable`, `test_report_never_claims_a_version_it_did_not_observe`).
Suite **1,948 / 158** (+3); fixtures re-verified byte-identical after the rewrite.

**CodeRabbit r2 — 2 Major applied (a third was lost to the rate limit; see below).** Both were the
same shape as r1's: a path that could fail **open**. (a) `rscape_version()` let
`subprocess.TimeoutExpired` and `OSError` escape while `backend_available()` caught only
`CovariationBackendError`, so a hung or unexecutable binary would have **crashed the readiness probe**
instead of refusing the round; every probe failure is now a `CovariationBackendError` and the version
probe has its own 60 s budget rather than inheriting the 15-minute run ceiling. (b) `run_rscape` read
`<outname>.helixcov` from a caller-supplied `outdir` **without checking it was fresh** — and R-scape
exits 0 without writing when an alignment carries no `SS_cons`, so a reused outdir would have parsed an
earlier candidate's file as this one's verdict (a stale `passed` on a candidate never scored). It now
refuses a pre-existing output outright. `run_rscape` also gained the same `OSError` arm.
Two more sabotage reverts confirm both bite by name. Suite **1,951 / 158** (+3).
**Honest gap:** CodeRabbit reported **3** findings in r2 and the CLI's incremental mode plus a Fair-Usage
rate limit (34-minute wait, escalating per the known pattern) meant the third was never re-read. The two
recovered were fixed; the third is unread, not dismissed, and the same fail-open class was self-audited
across the run path (that audit is what added `run_rscape`'s `OSError` arm, which CodeRabbit had only
flagged on the probe).

**Independent adversarial audit (6 agents, 0 failures) — 11 confirmed defects, all fixed.** Because
CodeRabbit's r2 reported 3 findings and the rate limit meant only 2 were readable, I ran a three-lens
read-only audit (fail-open paths / parser / tests) with a refuting verifier per lens. It found more than
the missing finding, including one that undercut this step's own headline claim.

**The one that mattered — the designed control's matchedness was asserted nowhere.** Both "the control
is matched" tests compared only R-scape's `# RM` helix geometry — and geometry is a function of
`#=GC SS_cons` and the gap pattern, which `make_rscape_fixture.py` copies **verbatim** into both arms.
So matchedness was guaranteed-looking by construction rather than checked: the suite stayed green with
the control `.sto` replaced by a copy of the positive, and with **both `.sto` files deleted outright**.
The gate's claim — *"a separation here can only be covariation"* — would have been false, and silently,
under any regeneration that preserved the gap pattern while destroying per-column composition. The
fixtures were verified to be genuinely matched (0/1460 columns differing); the property is now
**asserted** from the alignments themselves (identical ordered names, width, `SS_cons`/`RF`, per-column
`Counter`, N=60, and control ≠ positive), and `column_shuffle` — the function that *creates* the
matchedness — gained its own test file. This is the P2-10b lesson recurring inside the file that cites it.

**The second fail-open, one layer below the module's stated purpose.** Covariation detection is
power-limited, and R-scape reports an alignment too shallow to *show* covariation exactly as it reports
one that genuinely does not covary: all `nbp_cov = 0` ⇒ `failed` ⇒ **minable**. That is precisely the
"the backend failed this candidate" vs "nothing was ever evaluated" conflation `spare_rule.py` exists to
prevent, reintroduced at the layer below it — and this step's own sweep already measured the regime as
decisive (**0/12** subsamples passing at 5–8 sequences vs **12/12** at 60), with a real candidate's
homolog set landing in the low band. `covariation_verdict` now takes a keyword-required `min_sequences`
with **no default** (the same device as `AnyHelixCriterion`, and ADR-0006 D2's carve-out already
anticipates it — "with a minimum-covariation-power caveat reported"); below it the call raises, so the
caller records `unavailable` and the candidate is **spared**. `n_sequences` is recorded in every report
regardless, so a committed round can be re-graded against whatever floor is eventually pinned.
**The numeric value is a third open ADR-0006 item for P2-10e**, alongside the operator choice.

**Also fixed.** A multi-MSA input silently returned the *last* alignment's verdict (R-scape rewrites
`<outname>.helixcov` once per alignment) — reproduced end-to-end; now refused, plus `--onemsa`. The
`# RMs = N` header R-scape writes in every file was discarded instead of cross-checked against the
records parsed, so any record the regex missed deflated `nbp_cov` into a false `failed`. `as_report`
claimed an `evalue` the parsed text never evidenced — the *same* defect as the version stamp fixed in
r1, and `-E` could be deleted from the argv with every test still green. `text=True` let
`UnicodeDecodeError` escape the "every probe failure becomes `CovariationBackendError`" contract. Four
`run_rscape` guards had no test anywhere — including the version-mismatch refusal — and two tests used
bare `pytest.raises` that could not distinguish the branch they were named for.

**Two of my own new tests did not bite, and sabotage is why I know.** The row-shuffle discriminator
compared *sorted* per-row compositions — sorting throws away exactly the reordering it was meant to
detect, so a row permutation passed it. And the depth guard's only test lived in the ml tier, which
skips without the binary, so deleting the guard left bare CI green. Both rewritten; all three re-run
sabotages now fail the test **named** for the invariant. Suite **1,974 / 158** vs the like-for-like
`main` baseline **1,915 / 158** = **+59, zero regressions**; fixtures still byte-identical.

**CodeRabbit r3 — 2 findings, both applied; the review gate is closed.** (a) *Major:* fixture
publication was still not transactional — the r2 fix staged all four files in a temp dir but then moved
them into place **one at a time**, so an interruption partway through left a mixed-generation set on
disk: the same stale-control state the staging exists to prevent, merely relocated to a later moment.
Publication now backs up the current generation, moves all four, and **rolls the whole set back** if any
single move fails. (b) *Minor, and mine:* the row-shuffle test's second clause was written as
`<composition clause> or <the set clause asserted on the line above>` — satisfied by that earlier line
no matter what the composition clause said, i.e. an assertion that could never fail. It is now an
independent per-row-composition multiset comparison, and was re-sabotaged with the set clause deleted to
confirm it carries the row-shuffle detection **on its own**. Suite **1,974 / 158** unchanged; fixtures
byte-identical; ruff/black/`snakemake --lint`/DAG dry-run clean; all four GitHub Actions checks green.

## Step P2-10d — 2026-07-20: mined-negative injection + warm start in Stage-1 training

**Inputs.** `train_stage1::build_stream`/`build_model`/`_train_stage1_inner`, `conf/train/stage1.yaml`,
P2-01's `window_dataset` (`CorpusRecord`, `carve_window`, `WeightedIndexSampler`, `curriculum_weights`),
P2-10b's `data/processed/negatives/mining_pool_v0.parquet` (46,591 coordinate-bearing windows), P2-09's
round-0 production checkpoint `data/processed/checkpoints/stage1_production/stage1.pt`, PRD §9.1,
ADR-0005 D14.

**Process.** New module `src/tbox_finder/data/negatives.py`: `background_record()` builds an
all-background `CorpusRecord` whose pseudo-locus is co-extensive with the window, so a negative reaches
the model down the *same* carve as a positive and emits 1,024 real nucleotides labelled `background`
with no `IGNORE_INDEX` and no `[PAD]`; `MixedIndexSampler` interleaves negatives into the draw stream at
a configured share and `mix_summary()` **counts the emitted keys** to report the realized one;
`positive_only_sampler()` restricts the PRD §11 curriculum to the dataset's positive prefix and
*verifies* the boundary. In `train_stage1`: `warm_start()` loads a parent `state_dict` (`strict=True`)
and returns a per-tensor sha256 measurement taken around the load; `build_model` gained the warm-start
path and a fifth return value; `cfg.eval_at_step0` scores the P2-06a ladder before the first optimiser
step (placed before `reset_peak_memory_stats`, so eval activations stay out of the §10.3 training VRAM
figure); `batch_plan()` was split out of `_batches` so the mix is measured on the very keys the loop
consumes. Five new CLI-overridable fields landed in **both** the dataclass **and**
`conf/train/stage1.yaml`, gated by a new composition test.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/data/negatives.py` (new); `train_stage1` gains `warm_start`,
`_state_digests`, `batch_plan`, `ShardedSampler.inner`, report blocks `negative_mix{stream,consumed}` /
`warm_start` / `eval_metrics_step0` / `eval_scope_step0` / `step0_requested`, and two gate clauses
`negative_mix_realized` + `warm_start_loaded`; report `SCHEMA_VERSION` 1 → 2 with
`CLAUSE_SCHEMA_VERSION`; `reports/p2/negative_injection.json` (the measured substrate audit);
tests `tests/unit/test_negatives.py`, `tests/unit/test_p2_10d_clauses.py`, `tests/ml/test_warm_start.py`,
`tests/ml/test_negative_injection.py`, plus `test_sbatch_overrides.py::test_p2_10d_fields_are_config_keys_and_dataclass_fields`.

**Validation.** Both gates PASS, restated as exact identities rather than tolerances. **Mix:** the
counted negative keys must equal `round(n_positive · f/(1−f))` re-derived from the *counted* positive
keys — no tolerance knob exists to loosen — and the requested-zero branch asserts an empty pool and zero
draws rather than skipping. On the real corpus (`tests/ml/test_negative_injection.py`, 100 positives ×
200 negatives at f = 10/11) the emitted stream is exactly 100 / 1,000, every key counted negative
indexes a negative record, and the positive sub-stream is **identical** to the positives-only run's, so
P2-06's ranking and P2-09's checkpoint stay reproducible. **Warm start:** every model tensor byte-equal
to the parent's after the load (0 missing / 0 unexpected / 0 shape-mismatch), with two designed controls
— a fresh model must *not* match, and its forward must *differ* — and the warm-started forward is
bit-identical to the parent's, which is "reproduces its parent's metrics at step 0" in the only form
that is decidable without a GPU: identical parameters on identical inputs cannot yield different
metrics. The real P2-09 checkpoint's head keys and shapes fit the shipped `concat`/no-CRF skeleton.
Suite **2,017 passed / 189 skipped**, 2,206 collected vs `main`'s 2,132 = **+74, zero regressions**;
ruff + black clean.

**Decisions.** (1) *Negatives bypass the PRD §11 curriculum.* Routing them through it makes the realized
share `N^(1−3α)` — `N^0.25` at the shipped α = 0.25 — so a 10× larger pool moves the mix by only ~1.78×
while the config goes on stating a ratio the sampler contradicts. That is the `basis_point` defect in a
new costume, so the mix is a separate, counted draw stream and negatives carry a **private** stratum and
(negative) cluster namespace; a test measures that positive-vs-positive weights are unchanged to
`rtol = 1e-12`, with the shared-`UNKNOWN`-bucket control moving them by > 1e-3. (2) *Geometry is a
refusal.* A negative must be exactly one window of real DNA; padding a short one asserts a contig
boundary that does not exist and teaches "`[PAD]`-heavy ⇒ background", a cue absent from the scan
distribution. (3) *Schema versioning instead of regenerating artifacts.* Two new clauses would have
invalidated P2-04's and P2-09's committed reports; regenerating them to add the keys would forge
measurements those runs never made (§10.3). `validate_report` excuses a missing clause **only** for an
older `schema_version` **and only when it re-derives TRUE**, so an old report still cannot hide a
failure and a current-schema report must carry every clause — sabotaging either half fails the test
named for it.

**Measured blocker for P2-10e (this step's main empirical finding).** The hook lands; the 1,024-nt
substrate does not. `reports/p2/negative_injection.json`: of the mining pool's 46,591 rows, **0** are
injectable at the training window — 585 masked, 46,006 at 300 nt (`mining/pool.py::DEFAULT_WINDOW_NT`).
Re-carving wider does not fix it *at the shipped pad*: at margin 0 the corpus yields 45,519 carvable
1,024-nt flank windows, but at the D14 flank of 50 nt it yields **0**. P2-10e therefore needs a
wider-flank substrate (or a re-fetch), and `build_stream` refuses the current pool by name rather than
degrading to a positives-only run that reports a mix.

> **Correction (P2-10d′-a, 2026-07-21).** This paragraph originally read "P2-00 fetched **exactly
> 1,024 nt** of flank … a window that wide abuts its own parent locus and the union-prior mask removes
> every one." Both halves of the *cause* were wrong, and this step's own artifact contradicted the
> first: `reports/p2/negative_injection.json` recorded `max_trail_flank_nt: 1069`, with 8,898 rows
> carrying trail flank above 1,024. `DEFAULT_PAD_NT` pads the **named span**, and the anchored sequence
> is a strict sub-extent of it, so the realized flank routinely exceeds the pad. The per-record wall sat
> at margin **49** — a 5-nt miss, not arithmetic impossibility — and the mask claim was false for
> windows stitched across ≥ 2 fetched regions on one accession. The *conclusion* (no usable 1,024-nt
> substrate at the shipped pad) stands, and stood on scale rather than on impossibility. P2-10d′-a
> closed it by re-fetching at pad 1,074.

**Not decided here.** `negative_fraction` is a *stream share*, so PRD §9.1's "~10:1 seed ratio" reads as
f = 10/11; the shipped default is **0.0** and no round value is pinned. Pinning the round-0 fraction and
the round budget belongs to P2-10e's ADR-0005 amendment (CLAUDE.md §7 item 2), not to this step.

**Commit.** <SHA — see `git log` for `feat(train): mined-negative injection hook + warm-start path`>

**CodeRabbit r1 — 3 findings, all minor, all applied.** (a) The warm-start clause compared
`warm.checkpoint` to the raw config string while `warm_start` records `str(Path(...))`, so a config
spelt `./ckpt.pt` would have failed a load that in fact succeeded; both sides are now normalised, with
a test over three spellings plus a genuinely-different checkpoint. (b) The `max_records` break sat
*after* the append, so a cap of 0 emitted one record — a pool of size 1 beside a report saying
`max_records: 0`; moved before, with a test over caps 0/1/3/5/9. (c) `negative_mix_pool_consistent`
requires a non-empty draw whenever a fraction was requested, which a fraction small enough to round to
zero draws would have failed. CodeRabbit proposed relaxing the clause; I refused the *state* instead —
`MixedIndexSampler` now raises at construction, because a clause that admits a rounded-down zero also
admits a run that trained on no negatives at all while its report described a negative curriculum. The
boundary is tested in both directions (f = 0.001 over 10 positives refused; f = 0.05 accepted at
exactly 1 draw). Suite **2,020 / 189** (2,209 collected, **+77** vs `main`'s 2,132); ruff/black clean.

## Step P2-10d′-a — 2026-07-21: the wider-flank re-fetch, the 1,024-nt re-carve, and the `nested_train` parent-fold admission rule

**Inputs.** `data/interim/flank_context/context_v0.parquet` (23,535 rows, all `pad_nt = 1024`; the
resumable `fetch_cache.jsonl` was not on disk, so this was a full cold re-fetch of 23,535 NCBI requests,
not a top-up) · `workflow/rules/data.smk::p2_flank_context` · `conf/data/decoys.yaml` ·
`data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet` (git-LFS; PAR1 magic verified before use).

**Process.** Three changes, of which only the first is the one the step is named for and only the third
is the one that mattered. (1) `p2_flank_context` now passes `--pad-nt` — the flag has existed since
P2-00, the rule simply never passed it — with the value **derived** from the mining geometry
(`common.smk::flank_pad_nt` = `mining_window_nt + mining_margin_nt`) rather than restated, because a
duplicated pad drifts silently and the failure mode is an empty substrate rather than an error. The
helper lives in a new `workflow/rules/common.smk`: `snakemake --lint` refuses "Mixed rules and functions
in same snakefile", and a red lint is CI-blocking. (2) `conf/data/decoys.yaml` `mining_window_nt:
300 → 1024`, with `mining/pool.py::DEFAULT_WINDOW_NT` and `decoys._Config`'s fallback moved with it —
`read_config(None)` returns the bare object, so a default left at 300 would silently rebuild the pool at
a width from which no negative can be injected. (3) **The real deliverable.** `mining/pool.py` now joins
the committed split table and stamps every carved window with `parent_nested_train`, and
`negatives.load_negative_records` refuses any row whose parent is not in `nested_train`.

**Why (3) is the deliverable.** `background_record` stamps every injected negative `nested_train=True`
and `is_designated_loo_holdout=False`. Those are *assertions about the negative*, not checks on where its
DNA came from — and `genomic_window` is carved from the flank of **every** anchored corpus record,
held-out ones included. Measured on the shipped P2-10b pool: of 46,006 natural windows, **17,074
(37.1 %)** were carved beside a designated leave-one-order-out holdout locus, i.e. the immediate genomic
neighbourhood of the loci **GATE-4** grades, entering training stamped as training data. The CLAUDE.md
§8.2 CI gate is structurally blind to it — `test_no_leakage.py` reads the committed per-record split
table, and a runtime-injected negative appears in **no row of it**. The admission rule (user decision
2026-07-20) is symmetry with the positives: admissible iff the parent is in `nested_train`. Nothing
weaker is defensible — `excluded_clade_crossing` and `dropped` parents are withheld *because* their clade
membership is unsafe, so readmitting their DNA under a negative label readmits the taxon.

**Outputs.** `context_v0.parquet` re-fetched at pad **1,074** (`md5 ec214266…`, 23,535 rows) ·
`mining_pool_v0.parquet` re-carved at 1,024 nt with the new `parent_nested_train` column (`md5
c216409…`, **45,988** rows: lead 22,676 / trail 22,812 / control 500) · regenerated
`mining_pool_report.json`, `flank_context.json`, `negative_injection.json` · new
`tests/ml/test_negative_provenance.py` (the runtime gate) and `tests/unit/test_mining_window_config.py`
(the config↔code↔workflow pins, of which there were **none** — `grep mining_window_nt tests/` returned 0).

**Validation.** All five clauses of the step gate pass. `n_records` in `negative_injection.json`:
**0 → 15,708** injectable negatives at window 1,024. Designed control **500/500** with
`all_controls_exact` true (and overlap and masked). Natural residual contamination re-reported against
the union denominator **24,160** (24,157 loci loaded): **1,812/45,488 = 3.98 %**, up from 0.18 % at 300
nt — a 3.4× wider window has 3.4× more opportunity to intersect a masked locus, so the rise is
geometric, not a new contamination. **0** injectable negatives with a non-`nested_train` parent:
27,960 refused as `parent_not_nested_train`, **0** as `parent_fold_unknown` (all 23,132 distinct parents
resolved). Full-corpus no-leakage gate re-run as insurance: 25/25 green. Whole suite with every
`TBOX_REQUIRE_*` armed: **2,104 passed / 179 skipped / 0 failed**; `snakemake --lint`, `snakemake -n`,
black and ruff clean.

**Two measurements worth keeping.** (i) The first fetch pass lost **34** records to transient HTTP 429s
(`anchor_rate` 0.99847). The fetcher is resumable and retries `fetch_failed` by default, so a second
pass recovered all 34 and reproduced the committed composition **exactly**: 23,532 `ok` / 2 `multi_hit` /
1 `unavailable`, `anchor_rate` 0.99987. A wider pad did **not** cost anchoring — had the re-run been
skipped, the corpus would have shipped 33 records short with every test green. (ii) `n_full_window_capable`
is **23,253 at both pads**, and every P2-06a selection-val pin re-derives unchanged — 830 records / 469
blocks / 8,303 nested_train / 4,775 clusters / 7,472 inner_train. Training-window geometry is invariant
under the pad, so the P2-06 sweep and the P2-09 checkpoint remain comparable.

**Decisions.** The refusal lives at *load*, not at *carve*: out-of-fold windows stay in the artifact so
the natural-contamination denominator remains comparable across builds and the filter's own
discrimination is auditable from the artifact rather than only from the code that wrote it. The gate
therefore carries a **must-fire control** — `parent_fold_discriminates` requires
`n_parent_out_of_training_fold > 0`, gated on a number whose legitimate value can never be zero (the
corpus holds ~15.2k anchored non-`nested_train` records), so a join that silently matched nothing fails
loudly instead of reading as a clean corpus. Unresolved parents are counted and refused **apart** from
out-of-fold ones for the same reason. `CorpusRecord` gained a required `source_record_id` (no default,
for the reason `is_designated_loo_holdout` has none): the provenance test reads the parent as data
rather than parsing it out of an id string, and the fabricating default here — `record_id` — is right
for positives and silently wrong for exactly the records that matter.

**Sabotage.** The invariant test was proved to bite by name, not by suite colour. Admitting out-of-fold
parents (`if require_parent_nested_train and not bool(fold):` → `if False`) reddens
`^FAILED …::test_every_negative_build_stream_injects_is_training_fold_dna`; defaulting
`source_record_id` to the record's own id reddens the same test. Both restores verified byte-exact by
SHA-256 with `__pycache__` cleared and `-p no:cacheprovider`. The unit tier adds 23 further sabotage
cases across `mining/pool.py`, `decoys.py`, `data.smk` and `conf/data/decoys.yaml`, each matched to the
test named for it. The old blocker test was **rewritten, not deleted** — its `pytest.raises("supplied 0
injectable negatives")` is repointed at a deliberately 300-nt pool, so reverting the re-carve still goes
red.

**No ADR.** `pad_nt` is pinned nowhere (PRD §6 / ADR-0001 D31 / ADR-0005 D3 pin window = 1024 and
stride = 512; `flank_context.validate_report` enforces only `pad_nt >= window_nt`), and the parent-fold
filter *tightens* an existing pin (§9.2) rather than changing a number. §8.5's "can perturb cluster or
taxon membership" trigger is provably not met: clustering reads `FASTA_sequence` (`splits.py:406-412`),
`splits.py` has zero hits for flank/context, and `flank_context` *reads* the split table — splits are
upstream. The full-corpus gate was re-run anyway, and the selection-val pins above confirm it.

**Commit.** <SHA — see `git log` for `feat(mining): re-fetch flank at pad 1074 …`>
